title: The hardest question to answer about AI-fueled delusions
summary: This article explores the inherent challenges of AI systems generating false or misleading narratives, discussing implications for high-stakes contexts like military planning. It examines why AI models create convincing but unreliable outputs and the difficulty in addressing these "AI-fueled delusions."
content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. I was originally going to write this week’s newsletter about AI and Iran, particularly the news we broke last Tuesday that the Pentagon is making plans for AI companies to train on…

zh.title: 关于AI虚假信息最难回答的问题
zh.summary: 本文探讨了AI系统生成虚假或误导性叙述的固有挑战，讨论了对军事规划等高风险环境的影响。文章分析了为什么AI模型产生令人信服但不可靠的输出，以及解决这些"AI驱动的幻觉"的困难。

fr.title: La question la plus difficile à répondre sur les délires générés par l'IA
fr.summary: Cet article explore les défis inhérents des systèmes d'IA générant des récits faux ou trompeurs, en discutant des implications pour des contextes à enjeux élevés comme la planification militaire. Il examine pourquoi les modèles d'IA créent des résultats convaincants mais peu fiables et les difficultés à résoudre ces « délires générés par l'IA ».

de.title: Die schwierigste Frage zu KI-generierten Wahnvorstellungen beantworten
de.summary: Dieser Artikel untersucht die inhärenten Herausforderungen von KI-Systemen, die falsche oder irreführende Narrative erzeugen, und erörtert die Auswirkungen auf hochriskante Kontexte wie Militärplanung. Er analysiert, warum KI-Modelle überzeugende, aber unzuverlässige Ausgaben erzeugen und die Schwierigkeit, diese „KI-erzeugten Wahnvorstellungen" zu beheben.

es.title: La pregunta más difícil de responder sobre los delirios generados por IA
es.summary: Este artículo explora los desafíos inherentes de los sistemas de IA que generan narrativas falsas o engañosas, discutiendo las implicaciones para contextos de alto riesgo como la planificación militar. Examina por qué los modelos de IA crean resultados convincentes pero poco confiables y la dificultad de abordar estos « delirios generados por IA ».

title: The Download: animal welfare gets AGI-pilled, and the White House unveils its AI policy
summary: Animal welfare advocates are collaborating with AI researchers to apply advanced AI techniques to advocacy work, while the White House announces its official AI policy framework. The piece explores how traditionally non-technical sectors are adopting generative AI tools.
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The Bay Area’s animal welfare movement wants to recruit AI  In early February, animal welfare advocates and AI researchers arrived in stocking feet at Mox, a scrappy, shoes-free coworking space in…

zh.title: 下载：动物福利遇上AGI，白宫公布AI政策
zh.summary: 动物福利倡导者与AI研究人员合作，将先进的AI技术应用于倡导工作，同时白宫宣布其官方AI政策框架。这篇文章探讨了传统上非技术部门如何采用生成式AI工具。

fr.title: Le téléchargement : le bien-être animal devient AGI-conscious et la Maison Blanche dévoile sa politique IA
fr.summary: Les défenseurs du bien-être animal collaborent avec des chercheurs en IA pour appliquer des techniques d'IA avancées au travail de plaidoyer, tandis que la Maison Blanche annonce son cadre officiel de politique en matière d'IA. L'article explore comment les secteurs traditionnellement non techniques adoptent les outils d'IA générative.

de.title: Der Download: Tierschutz wird AGI-bewusst und das Weiße Haus enthüllt seine KI-Politik
de.summary: Tierschutzaktivisten arbeiten mit KI-Forschern zusammen, um fortgeschrittene KI-Techniken auf Advocacy-Arbeit anzuwenden, während das Weiße Haus sein offizielles KI-Politikrahmenwerk ankündigt. Der Artikel untersucht, wie traditionell nichttechnische Sektoren generative KI-Tools übernehmen.

es.title: La descarga: el bienestar animal se vuelve AGI-consciente y la Casa Blanca revela su política de IA
es.summary: Los defensores del bienestar animal colaboran con investigadores de IA para aplicar técnicas avanzadas de IA al trabajo de defensa, mientras que la Casa Blanca anuncia su marco oficial de política de IA. El artículo explora cómo los sectores tradicionalmente no técnicos están adoptando herramientas de IA generativa.

title: The Bay Area’s animal welfare movement wants to recruit AI
summary: Animal welfare advocates and AI researchers are collaborating to explore how artificial intelligence can support animal welfare and conservation efforts. The partnership reflects emerging interest in applying AI expertise to address challenges in protecting animal welfare.
content: In early February, animal welfare advocates and AI researchers gathered in stocking feet at Mox, a scrappy, shoes-free coworking space in San Francisco. Yellow and red canopies billowed overhead, Persian rugs blanketed the floor, and mosaic lamps glowed beside potted plants.  In the common area, a wildlife advocate spoke passionately to a crowd lounging in…

zh.title: 湾区动物福利运动想要招募AI
zh.summary: 动物福利倡导者和人工智能研究人员正在合作探索人工智能如何支持动物福利和保护工作。这一合作反映了人们对利用AI专业知识解决保护动物福利挑战的兴趣日益增长。

fr.title: Le mouvement de bien-être animal de la Bay Area veut recruter l'IA
fr.summary: Les défenseurs du bien-être animal et les chercheurs en intelligence artificielle collaborent pour explorer comment l'intelligence artificielle peut soutenir le bien-être animal et les efforts de conservation. Le partenariat reflète l'intérêt croissant d'appliquer l'expertise en IA pour relever les défis de la protection du bien-être animal.

de.title: Die Tierschutzbewegung der Bay Area möchte KI rekrutieren
de.summary: Tierwohlverfechter und KI-Forscher arbeiten zusammen, um zu erkunden, wie künstliche Intelligenz Tierschutz und Schutzmaßnahmen unterstützen kann. Die Partnerschaft spiegelt das wachsende Interesse wider, KI-Expertise einzusetzen, um Herausforderungen beim Schutz des Tierwohls zu bewältigen.

es.title: El movimiento de bienestar animal del Área de la Bahía quiere reclutar IA
es.summary: Los defensores del bienestar animal y los investigadores de inteligencia artificial colaboran para explorar cómo la inteligencia artificial puede apoyar el bienestar animal y los esfuerzos de conservación. La asociación refleja el interés creciente en aplicar la experiencia en IA para abordar los desafíos en la protección del bienestar animal.

title: When both Grounding and not Grounding are Bad -- A Partially Grounded Encoding of Planning into SAT (Extended Version)
summary: Researchers propose a middle ground between fully lifted and fully grounded planning representations, introducing three SAT encodings that keep actions lifted while partially grounding predicates. This approach scales linearly with plan length instead of quadratically, outperforming state-of-the-art methods on hard-to-ground domains.
content:
arXiv:2603.19429v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Classical planning problems are typically defined using lifted first-order representations, which offer compactness and generality. While most planners ground these representations to simplify reasoning, this can cause an exponential blowup in size. Recent approaches instead operate directly on the lifted level to avoid full grounding. We explore a middle ground between fully lifted and fully grounded planning by introducing three SAT encodings that keep actions lifted while partially grounding predicates. Unlike previous SAT encodings, which scale quadratically with plan length, our approach scales linearly, enabling better performance on longer plans. Empirically, our best encoding outperforms the state of the art in length-optimal planning on hard-to-ground domains.

zh.title: 当接地和不接地都不好时——规划到SAT的部分接地编码（扩展版）
zh.summary: 研究人员在完全提升和完全接地的规划表示之间提出了一个中间立场，引入三种SAT编码，保持动作提升的同时部分接地谓词。这种方法相对于计划长度呈线性扩展，而不是二次方，在难以接地的领域中优于最先进的方法。

fr.title: Quand l'ancrage et le non-ancrage sont mauvais -- Un encodage partiellement ancré de la planification dans SAT (Version étendue)
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent un juste milieu entre les représentations de planification entièrement levées et entièrement ancrées, en introduisant trois encodages SAT qui conservent les actions levées tout en ancrant partiellement les prédicats. Cette approche se met à l'échelle linéairement avec la longueur du plan au lieu de quadratiquement, surpassant les méthodes de l'art pour les domaines difficiles à ancrer.

de.title: Wenn sowohl Verankern als auch Nicht-Verankern schlecht sind -- Eine teilweise verankerte Kodierung der Planung in SAT (Erweiterte Version)
de.summary: Forscher schlagen einen Mittelweg zwischen vollständig gehobenen und vollständig verankerten Planungsdarstellungen vor und führen drei SAT-Kodierungen ein, die Aktionen heben, während sie Prädikate teilweise verankern. Dieser Ansatz skaliert linear mit der Planlänge anstatt quadratisch und übertrifft modernste Methoden in schwer zu verankernden Domänen.

es.title: Cuando tanto fundamentación como no fundamentación son malas -- Una codificación parcialmente fundamentada de la planificación en SAT (Versión extendida)
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen un término medio entre representaciones de planificación completamente elevadas y completamente fundamentadas, introduciendo tres codificaciones SAT que mantienen acciones elevadas mientras fundamentan parcialmente predicados. Este enfoque se escala linealmente con la longitud del plan en lugar de cuadráticamente, superando a los métodos de última generación en dominios difíciles de fundamentar.

title: Hyperagents
summary: Introduces hyperagents, self-improving AI systems where both the task-solving agent and meta-modification mechanism are editable, enabling metacognitive self-improvement. DGM-Hyperagents demonstrate that this approach can improve performance across diverse domains while developing better improvement procedures that transfer across tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.19461v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Self-improving AI systems aim to reduce reliance on human engineering by learning to improve their own learning and problem-solving processes. Existing approaches to self-improvement rely on fixed, handcrafted meta-level mechanisms, fundamentally limiting how fast such systems can improve. The Darwin G\"odel Machine (DGM) demonstrates open-ended self-improvement in coding by repeatedly generating and evaluating self-modified variants. Because both evaluation and self-modification are coding tasks, gains in coding ability can translate into gains in self-improvement ability. However, this alignment does not generally hold beyond coding domains. We introduce \textbf{hyperagents}, self-referential agents that integrate a task agent (which solves the target task) and a meta agent (which modifies itself and the task agent) into a single editable program. Crucially, the meta-level modification procedure is itself editable, enabling metacognitive self-modification, improving not only the task-solving behavior, but also the mechanism that generates future improvements. We instantiate this framework by extending DGM to create DGM-Hyperagents (DGM-H), eliminating the assumption of domain-specific alignment between task performance and self-modification skill to potentially support self-accelerating progress on any computable task. Across diverse domains, the DGM-H improves performance over time and outperforms baselines without self-improvement or open-ended exploration, as well as prior self-improving systems. Furthermore, the DGM-H improves the process by which it generates new agents (e.g., persistent memory, performance tracking), and these meta-level improvements transfer across domains and accumulate across runs. DGM-Hyperagents offer a glimpse of open-ended AI systems that do not merely search for better solutions, but continually improve their search for how to improve.

zh.title: 超智能体
zh.summary: 介绍超智能体，自我改进的AI系统，其中任务解决智能体和元修改机制都是可编辑的，从而能够进行元认知自我改进。DGM-超智能体证明这种方法可以在不同领域提高性能，同时开发出可跨任务转移的更好改进程序。

fr.title: Hyperagents
fr.summary: Introduit les hyperagents, des systèmes d'IA auto-améliorés où l'agent résolvant les tâches et le mécanisme de modification sont éditables, permettant l'auto-amélioration métacognitive. Les DGM-Hyperagents démontrent que cette approche peut améliorer les performances dans des domaines divers tout en développant de meilleures procédures d'amélioration qui se transfèrent entre les tâches.

de.title: Hyperagenten
de.summary: Stellt Hyperagenten vor, selbstverbessernde KI-Systeme, bei denen sowohl der aufgabenlösende Agent als auch der Metaänderungsmechanismus bearbeitbar sind, was metakognitive Selbstverbesserung ermöglicht. DGM-Hyperagenten zeigen, dass dieser Ansatz die Leistung in verschiedenen Bereichen verbessern kann und gleichzeitig bessere Verbesserungsverfahren entwickelt, die über Aufgaben hinweg übertragbar sind.

es.title: Hiperagentes
es.summary: Introduce hiperagentes, sistemas de IA auto-mejorables donde tanto el agente resolvedor de tareas como el mecanismo de modificación son editables, permitiendo la auto-mejora metacognitiva. Los DGM-Hiperagentes demuestran que este enfoque puede mejorar el rendimiento en dominios diversos mientras desarrolla mejores procedimientos de mejora que se transfieren entre tareas.

title: Teaching an Agent to Sketch One Part at a Time
summary: Presents a method for generating vector sketches part-by-part using a multi-modal language model with process-reward reinforcement learning, supported by a new ControlSketch-Part dataset with semantic part-level annotations. The approach enables interpretable, controllable, and locally editable text-to-sketch generation.
content:
arXiv:2603.19500v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We develop a method for producing vector sketches one part at a time. To do this, we train a multi-modal language model-based agent using a novel multi-turn process-reward reinforcement learning following supervised fine-tuning. Our approach is enabled by a new dataset we call ControlSketch-Part, containing rich part-level annotations for sketches, obtained using a novel, generic automatic annotation pipeline that segments vector sketches into semantic parts and assigns paths to parts with a structured multi-stage labeling process. Our results indicate that incorporating structured part-level data and providing agent with the visual feedback through the process enables interpretable, controllable, and locally editable text-to-vector sketch generation.

zh.title: 教一个代理逐部分绘制草图
zh.summary: 提出了一种使用多模态语言模型和流程奖励强化学习逐部分生成向量草图的方法，由带有语义部分级注释的新ControlSketch-Part数据集支持。该方法支持可解释、可控和局部可编辑的文本到草图生成。

fr.title: Apprendre à un Agent à Esquisser une Partie à la Fois
fr.summary: Présente une méthode pour générer des croquis vectoriels partie par partie en utilisant un modèle de langage multimodal avec apprentissage par renforcement basé sur les récompenses de processus, soutenu par un nouvel ensemble de données ControlSketch-Part avec des annotations au niveau sémantique des parties. L'approche permet une génération de texte en esquisse interprétable, contrôlable et localement modifiable.

de.title: Einen Agenten lehren, eine Skizze Teil für Teil zu erstellen
de.summary: Präsentiert eine Methode zum Generieren von Vektorskizzen Teil für Teil mit einem multimodalen Sprachmodell und Prozessbelohnungs-Reinforcement-Learning, unterstützt durch einen neuen ControlSketch-Part-Datensatz mit semantischen Anmerkungen auf Teilebene. Der Ansatz ermöglicht interpretierbare, steuerbare und lokal bearbeitbare Text-zu-Skizze-Generierung.

es.title: Enseñar a un Agente a Dibujar una Parte a la Vez
es.summary: Presenta un método para generar esquemas vectoriales parte por parte utilizando un modelo de lenguaje multimodal con aprendizaje reforzado basado en recompensas de proceso, apoyado por un nuevo conjunto de datos ControlSketch-Part con anotaciones semánticas a nivel de parte. El enfoque permite la generación de texto a esquema interpretable, controlable y localmente editable.

title: Learning to Disprove: Formal Counterexample Generation with Large Language Models
summary: Addresses the neglected task of counterexample generation in AI mathematics by fine-tuning LLMs to produce formal counterexamples verifiable in Lean 4. A symbolic mutation strategy creates diverse training data, and the multi-reward expert iteration framework significantly improves both counterexample generation and theorem proving performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.19514v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Mathematical reasoning demands two critical, complementary skills: constructing rigorous proofs for true statements and discovering counterexamples that disprove false ones. However, current AI efforts in mathematics focus almost exclusively on proof construction, often neglecting the equally important task of finding counterexamples. In this paper, we address this gap by fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) to reason about and generate counterexamples. We formalize this task as formal counterexample generation, which requires LLMs not only to propose candidate counterexamples but also to produce formal proofs that can be automatically verified in the Lean 4 theorem prover. To enable effective learning, we introduce a symbolic mutation strategy that synthesizes diverse training data by systematically extracting theorems and discarding selected hypotheses, thereby producing diverse counterexample instances. Together with curated datasets, this strategy enables a multi-reward expert iteration framework that substantially enhances both the effectiveness and efficiency of training LLMs for counterexample generation and theorem proving. Experiments on three newly collected benchmarks validate the advantages of our approach, showing that the mutation strategy and training framework yield significant performance gains.

zh.title: 学习反驳：用大型语言模型生成形式反例
zh.summary: 通过微调大型语言模型来解决AI数学中被忽视的反例生成任务，生成可在Lean 4中验证的形式反例。符号变异策略创建多样化的训练数据，多奖励专家迭代框架显著改进了反例生成和定理证明性能。

fr.title: Apprendre à Réfuter : Génération de Contre-exemples Formels avec des Grands Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: Aborde la tâche négligée de la génération de contre-exemples en mathématiques IA en affinant les LLM pour produire des contre-exemples formels vérifiables dans Lean 4. Une stratégie de mutation symbolique crée des données d'entraînement diversifiées, et le cadre d'itération d'expert multi-récompense améliore considérablement à la fois la génération de contre-exemples et les performances de preuve de théorème.

de.title: Lernen zu Widersprechen: Formale Gegenbeispielgenerierung mit großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Befasst sich mit der vernachlässigten Aufgabe der Gegenbeispielgenerierung in der KI-Mathematik durch Feinabstimmung von LLMs zur Erzeugung formaler Gegenbeispiele, die in Lean 4 überprüfbar sind. Eine symbolische Mutationsstrategie erstellt vielfältige Trainingsdaten, und das Multi-Reward-Expert-Iterations-Framework verbessert erheblich sowohl die Gegenbeispielgenerierung als auch die Theorem-Proving-Leistung.

es.title: Aprender a Refutar: Generación de Contraejemplos Formales con Modelos de Lenguaje Grande
es.summary: Aborda la tarea descuidada de la generación de contraejemplos en matemáticas de IA ajustando LLM para producir contraejemplos formales verificables en Lean 4. Una estrategia de mutación simbólica crea datos de entrenamiento diversos, y el marco de iteración de expertos multi-recompensa mejora significativamente tanto la generación de contraejemplos como el desempeño de la prueba de teoremas.

title: ItinBench: Benchmarking Planning Across Multiple Cognitive Dimensions with Large Language Models
summary: Introduces ItinBench, a benchmark that evaluates LLMs across multiple cognitive dimensions including spatial reasoning and verbal reasoning through trip itinerary planning. The study reveals that state-of-the-art LLMs struggle with consistent performance across multiple cognitive domains simultaneously.
content:
arXiv:2603.19515v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) with advanced cognitive capabilities are emerging as agents for various reasoning and planning tasks. Traditional evaluations often focus on specific reasoning or planning questions within controlled environments. Recent studies have explored travel planning as a medium to integrate various verbal reasoning tasks into real-world contexts. However, reasoning tasks extend beyond verbal reasoning alone, and a comprehensive evaluation of LLMs requires a testbed that incorporates tasks from multiple cognitive domains. To address this gap, we introduce ItinBench, a benchmark that features one task of spatial reasoning, i.e., route optimization, into trip itinerary planning while keeping the traditional verbal reasoning tasks. ItinBench evaluates various LLMs across diverse tasks simultaneously, including Llama 3.1 8B, Mistral Large, Gemini 1.5 Pro, and GPT family. Our findings reveal that LLMs struggle to maintain high and consistent performance when concurrently handling multiple cognitive dimensions. By incorporating tasks from distinct human-level cognitive domains, ItinBench provides new insights into building more comprehensive reasoning testbeds that better reflect real-world challenges. The code and dataset: https://ethanwtl.github.io/IBweb/

zh.title: ItinBench：用大型语言模型对多个认知维度的规划进行基准测试
zh.summary: 介绍了ItinBench，一个基准测试，通过旅行行程规划评估大型语言模型在多个认知维度（包括空间推理和语言推理）的能力。研究表明，最先进的大型语言模型在同时应对多个认知领域时性能表现不一致。

fr.title: ItinBench : Benchmarking de la Planification à Travers Plusieurs Dimensions Cognitives avec des Grands Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: Introduit ItinBench, un benchmark qui évalue les LLM à travers plusieurs dimensions cognitives incluant le raisonnement spatial et le raisonnement verbal par la planification d'itinéraires de voyage. L'étude révèle que les LLM de pointe ont du mal avec des performances cohérentes dans plusieurs domaines cognitifs simultanément.

de.title: ItinBench: Benchmarking der Planung über mehrere kognitive Dimensionen mit großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Führt ItinBench ein, einen Benchmark, der LLMs über mehrere kognitive Dimensionen einschließlich räumliches Denken und verbales Denken durch Reiseplanung bewertet. Die Studie zeigt, dass hochmoderne LLMs mit konsistenter Leistung über mehrere kognitive Domänen gleichzeitig kämpfen.

es.title: ItinBench: Evaluación Comparativa de Planificación en Múltiples Dimensiones Cognitivas con Modelos de Lenguaje Grande
es.summary: Introduce ItinBench, un punto de referencia que evalúa los LLM en múltiples dimensiones cognitivas incluyendo razonamiento espacial y razonamiento verbal a través de la planificación de itinerarios de viaje. El estudio revela que los LLM más avanzados luchan por mantener un desempeño consistente en múltiples dominios cognitivos simultáneamente.

title: PA2D-MORL: Pareto Ascent Directional Decomposition based Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning
summary: Proposes PA2D-MORL, a multi-objective reinforcement learning method that uses Pareto ascent direction for weight selection and policy improvement. The approach achieves superior approximations of Pareto policy sets through selective policy optimization under an evolutionary framework and adaptive fine-tuning.
content:
arXiv:2603.19579v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-objective reinforcement learning (MORL) provides an effective solution for decision-making problems involving conflicting objectives. However, achieving high-quality approximations to the Pareto policy set remains challenging, especially in complex tasks with continuous or high-dimensional state-action space. In this paper, we propose the Pareto Ascent Directional Decomposition based Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (PA2D-MORL) method, which constructs an efficient scheme for multi-objective problem decomposition and policy improvement, leading to a superior approximation of Pareto policy set. The proposed method leverages Pareto ascent direction to select the scalarization weights and computes the multi-objective policy gradient, which determines the policy optimization direction and ensures joint improvement on all objectives. Meanwhile, multiple policies are selectively optimized under an evolutionary framework to approximate the Pareto frontier from different directions. Additionally, a Pareto adaptive fine-tuning approach is applied to enhance the density and spread of the Pareto frontier approximation. Experiments on various multi-objective robot control tasks show that the proposed method clearly outperforms the current state-of-the-art algorithm in terms of both quality and stability of the outcomes.

zh.title: PA2D-MORL：基于帕累托上升方向分解的多目标强化学习
zh.summary: 提出了PA2D-MORL，一种多目标强化学习方法，使用帕累托上升方向进行权重选择和策略改进。该方法通过进化框架下的选择性策略优化和自适应微调，实现了帕累托策略集的优越近似。

fr.title: PA2D-MORL : Apprentissage par Renforcement Multi-Objectif Basé sur la Décomposition Directionnelle de l'Ascension de Pareto
fr.summary: Propose PA2D-MORL, une méthode d'apprentissage par renforcement multi-objectif qui utilise la direction d'ascension de Pareto pour la sélection des poids et l'amélioration des politiques. L'approche réalise des approximations supérieures des ensembles de politiques de Pareto grâce à l'optimisation de politique sélective dans un cadre évolutionnaire et l'ajustement fin adaptatif.

de.title: PA2D-MORL: Mehrzieliges Verstärkungslernverfahren basierend auf Pareto-Aufstiegrichtungs-Dekomposition
de.summary: Schlägt PA2D-MORL vor, eine Methode des mehrzieligen Verstärkungslernens, die die Pareto-Aufstiegsrichtung für die Gewichtsauswahl und Politikverbesserung nutzt. Der Ansatz erreicht überlegene Annäherungen von Pareto-Politiksätzen durch selektive Politikoptimierung in einem evolutionären Rahmen und adaptive Feinabstimmung.

es.title: PA2D-MORL: Aprendizaje por Refuerzo Multiobjetivo Basado en Descomposición Direccional de Ascenso de Pareto
es.summary: Propone PA2D-MORL, un método de aprendizaje por refuerzo multiobjetivo que utiliza la dirección de ascenso de Pareto para la selección de pesos y mejora de políticas. El enfoque logra aproximaciones superiores de conjuntos de políticas de Pareto mediante optimización de políticas selectiva bajo un marco evolutivo y ajuste fino adaptativo.

title: PowerLens: Taming LLM Agents for Safe and Personalized Mobile Power Management
summary: Presents PowerLens, a system leveraging LLM reasoning for context-aware battery management on Android devices. Using PDL-based constraints for safety and implicit feedback learning, the system achieves 38.8% energy savings and 81.7% action accuracy without explicit user configuration.
content:
arXiv:2603.19584v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Battery life remains a critical challenge for mobile devices, yet existing power management mechanisms rely on static rules or coarse-grained heuristics that ignore user activities and personal preferences. We present PowerLens, a system that tames the reasoning power of Large Language Models (LLMs) for safe and personalized mobile power management on Android devices. The key idea is that LLMs' commonsense reasoning can bridge the semantic gap between user activities and system parameters, enabling zero-shot, context-aware policy generation that adapts to individual preferences through implicit feedback. PowerLens employs a multi-agent architecture that recognizes user context from UI semantics and generates holistic power policies across 18 device parameters. A PDL-based constraint framework verifies every action before execution, while a two-tier memory system learns individualized preferences from implicit user overrides through confidence-based distillation, requiring no explicit configuration and converging within 3--5 days. Extensive experiments on a rooted Android device show that PowerLens achieves 81.7% action accuracy and 38.8% energy saving over stock Android, outperforming rule-based and LLM-based baselines, with high user satisfaction, fast preference convergence, and strong safety guarantees, with the system itself consuming only 0.5% of daily battery capacity.

zh.title: PowerLens：驯服LLM代理以实现安全和个性化的移动电源管理
zh.summary: 介绍PowerLens，一个利用LLM推理实现Android设备上下文感知电池管理的系统。使用基于PDL的约束来确保安全性和隐式反馈学习，该系统在没有明确用户配置的情况下实现了38.8%的能源节省和81.7%的行动准确率。

fr.title: PowerLens : Maîtriser les Agents LLM pour une Gestion de l'Énergie Mobile Sûre et Personnalisée
fr.summary: Présente PowerLens, un système exploitant le raisonnement des LLM pour une gestion contextuelle de la batterie sur les appareils Android. Utilisant des contraintes basées sur PDL pour la sécurité et l'apprentissage par retour implicite, le système réalise 38,8% d'économies d'énergie et 81,7% de précision des actions sans configuration utilisateur explicite.

de.title: PowerLens: Zähmung von LLM-Agenten für sichere und personalisierte Mobile Stromsparung
de.summary: Stellt PowerLens vor, ein System, das LLM-Reasoning für kontextbewusste Batterienverwaltung auf Android-Geräten nutzt. Mit PDL-basierten Einschränkungen für Sicherheit und implizites Feedback-Lernen erreicht das System 38,8% Energieeinsparungen und 81,7% Aktionsgenauigkeit ohne explizite Benutzerkonfiguration.

es.title: PowerLens: Domando Agentes LLM para una Gestión de Energía Móvil Segura y Personalizada
es.summary: Presenta PowerLens, un sistema que aprovecha el razonamiento de LLM para la gestión contextual de batería en dispositivos Android. Utilizando restricciones basadas en PDL para la seguridad y aprendizaje de retroalimentación implícita, el sistema logra un 38,8% de ahorro de energía y una precisión de acción del 81,7% sin configuración explícita del usuario.

title: HyEvo: Self-Evolving Hybrid Agentic Workflows for Efficient Reasoning
summary: Proposes HyEvo, a workflow generation framework combining probabilistic LLM nodes with deterministic code nodes to reduce inference costs. The hybrid approach employs multi-island evolutionary search, achieving up to 19× reduction in inference cost and 16× in execution latency compared to baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.19639v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Although agentic workflows have demonstrated strong potential for solving complex tasks, existing automated generation methods remain inefficient and underperform, as they rely on predefined operator libraries and homogeneous LLM-only workflows in which all task-level computation is performed through probabilistic inference. To address these limitations, we propose HyEvo, an automated workflow-generation framework that leverages heterogeneous atomic synthesis. HyEvo integrates probabilistic LLM nodes for semantic reasoning with deterministic code nodes for rule-based execution, offloading predictable operations from LLM inference and reducing inference cost and execution latency. To efficiently navigate the hybrid search space, HyEvo employs an LLM-driven multi-island evolutionary strategy with a reflect-then-generate mechanism, iteratively refining both workflow topology and node logic via execution feedback. Comprehensive experiments show that HyEvo consistently outperforms existing methods across diverse reasoning and coding benchmarks, while reducing inference cost and execution latency by up to 19$\times$ and 16$\times$, respectively, compared to the state-of-the-art open-source baseline.

zh.title: HyEvo：用于高效推理的自演化混合代理工作流
zh.summary: 提出HyEvo，一个工作流生成框架，结合概率性LLM节点和确定性代码节点来降低推理成本。该混合方法采用多岛屿进化搜索，相比基线实现了高达19倍的推理成本降低和16倍的执行延迟降低。

fr.title: HyEvo : Flux de Travail d'Agents Hybrides Auto-Évolutifs pour un Raisonnement Efficace
fr.summary: Propose HyEvo, un cadre de génération de flux de travail combinant des nœuds LLM probabilistes avec des nœuds de code déterministes pour réduire les coûts d'inférence. L'approche hybride emploie une recherche évolutionnaire multi-îles, réalisant jusqu'à une réduction de 19× du coût d'inférence et de 16× de la latence d'exécution par rapport aux lignes de base.

de.title: HyEvo: Selbst-evolvierende Hybrid-Agenten-Workflows für effizientes Denken
de.summary: Schlägt HyEvo vor, ein Workflow-Generierungsframework, das probabilistische LLM-Knoten mit deterministischen Code-Knoten kombiniert, um Inferenzkosten zu senken. Der Hybrid-Ansatz nutzt Multi-Island-Evolutionssuche und erreicht bis zu 19× Reduktion der Inferenzkosten und 16× der Ausführungslatenz im Vergleich zu Baselines.

es.title: HyEvo: Flujos de Trabajo de Agentes Híbridos Auto-Evolucionantes para Razonamiento Eficiente
es.summary: Propone HyEvo, un marco de generación de flujos de trabajo que combina nodos LLM probabilísticos con nodos de código deterministas para reducir costos de inferencia. El enfoque híbrido emplea búsqueda evolutiva multi-isla, logrando hasta 19× de reducción en costo de inferencia y 16× en latencia de ejecución en comparación con líneas base.

title: A Subgoal-driven Framework for Improving Long-Horizon LLM Agents
summary: Addresses long-horizon planning challenges in LLM agents through subgoal decomposition and MiRA, an RL framework using milestone-based dense rewards. The approach enables Gemma3-12B to achieve 43.0% success on WebArena-Lite, surpassing proprietary systems like GPT-4-Turbo.
content:
arXiv:2603.19685v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based agents have emerged as powerful autonomous controllers for digital environments, including mobile interfaces, operating systems, and web browsers. Web navigation, for example, requires handling dynamic content and long sequences of actions, making it particularly challenging. Existing LLM-based agents struggle with long-horizon planning in two main ways. During online execution, they often lose track as new information arrives, lacking a clear and adaptive path toward the final goal. This issue is further exacerbated during reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tuning, where sparse and delayed rewards make it difficult for agents to identify which actions lead to success, preventing them from maintaining coherent reasoning over extended tasks. To address these challenges, we propose two contributions. First, we introduce an agent framework that leverages proprietary models for online planning through subgoal decomposition. Second, we present MiRA (Milestoning your Reinforcement Learning Enhanced Agent), an RL training framework that uses dense, milestone-based reward signals. The real-time planning mechanism improves proprietary models such as Gemini by approximately a 10% absolute increase in success rate (SR) on the WebArena-Lite benchmark. Meanwhile, applying MiRA to the open Gemma3-12B model increases its success rate from 6.4% to 43.0%. This performance surpasses proprietary systems such as GPT-4-Turbo (17.6%) and GPT-4o (13.9%), as well as the previous open-model state of the art, WebRL (38.4%). Overall, our findings demonstrate that combining explicit inference-time planning with milestone-based rewards significantly improves an agent's long-horizon capabilities, paving the way for more robust and general-purpose autonomous systems.

zh.title: 用于改进长视野LLM代理的子目标驱动框架
zh.summary: 通过子目标分解和MiRA来解决LLM代理中的长视野规划挑战，MiRA是一个使用基于里程碑的密集奖励的强化学习框架。该方法使Gemma3-12B在WebArena-Lite上实现43.0%的成功率，超过了GPT-4-Turbo等专有系统。

fr.title: Un Cadre Piloté par Sous-Objectifs pour Améliorer les Agents LLM à Horizon Long
fr.summary: Aborde les défis de planification à horizon long dans les agents LLM grâce à la décomposition des sous-objectifs et MiRA, un cadre RL utilisant des récompenses denses basées sur les jalons. L'approche permet à Gemma3-12B d'atteindre 43,0% de succès sur WebArena-Lite, surpassant les systèmes propriétaires comme GPT-4-Turbo.

de.title: Ein Subziel-gesteuertes Framework zur Verbesserung von Langziel-LLM-Agenten
de.summary: Befasst sich mit Herausforderungen der Langzeitplanung bei LLM-Agenten durch Subziel-Zerlegung und MiRA, ein RL-Framework mit meilensteinbasierten dichten Belohnungen. Der Ansatz ermöglicht Gemma3-12B, 43,0% Erfolg auf WebArena-Lite zu erreichen und übertrifft proprietäre Systeme wie GPT-4-Turbo.

es.title: Un Marco Impulsado por Subobjetivos para Mejorar Agentes LLM de Horizonte Largo
es.summary: Aborda los desafíos de planificación a largo horizonte en agentes LLM mediante descomposición de subobjetivos y MiRA, un marco de RL que utiliza recompensas densas basadas en hitos. El enfoque permite que Gemma3-12B logre un 43,0% de éxito en WebArena-Lite, superando sistemas propietarios como GPT-4-Turbo.

title: Stepwise: Neuro-Symbolic Proof Search for Automated Systems Verification
summary: Introduces Stepwise, a neuro-symbolic proof generation framework for automated systems verification that combines fine-tuned LLMs with interactive theorem prover tools. The approach achieves 77.6% theorem proof rate on the seL4 benchmark, substantially surpassing previous LLM-based methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.19715v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Formal verification via interactive theorem proving is increasingly used to ensure the correctness of critical systems, yet constructing large proof scripts remains highly manual and limits scalability. Advances in large language models (LLMs), especially in mathematical reasoning, make their integration into software verification increasingly promising. This paper introduces a neuro-symbolic proof generation framework designed to automate proof search for systems-level verification projects. The framework performs a best-first tree search over proof states, repeatedly querying an LLM for the next candidate proof step. On the neural side, we fine-tune LLMs using datasets of proof state-step pairs; on the symbolic side, we incorporate a range of ITP tools to repair rejected steps, filter and rank proof states, and automatically discharge subgoals when search progress stalls. This synergy enables data-efficient LLM adaptation and semantics-informed pruning of the search space. We implement the framework on a new Isabelle REPL that exposes fine-grained proof states and automation tools, and evaluate it on the FVEL seL4 benchmark and additional Isabelle developments. On seL4, the system proves up to 77.6\% of the theorems, substantially surpassing previous LLM-based approaches and standalone Sledgehammer, while solving significantly more multi-step proofs. Results across further benchmarks demonstrate strong generalization, indicating a viable path toward scalable automated software verification.

zh.title: Stepwise: 神经符号证明搜索用于自动系统验证
zh.summary: 介绍Stepwise，一个神经符号证明生成框架，用于自动系统验证，将微调的大语言模型与交互式定理证明工具相结合。该方法在seL4基准上实现了77.6%的定理证明率，大幅超越了之前基于大语言模型的方法。

fr.title: Stepwise: Recherche de preuve neuro-symbolique pour la vérification automatisée des systèmes
fr.summary: Présente Stepwise, un cadre de génération de preuve neuro-symbolique pour la vérification automatisée des systèmes qui combine des modèles de langage affinés avec des outils de prouveur de théorèmes interactifs. L'approche atteint un taux de preuve de théorème de 77,6% sur l'indice de référence seL4, surpassant considérablement les méthodes antérieures basées sur les modèles de langage.

de.title: Stepwise: Neuro-symbolische Beweissuche für automatisierte Systemverifikation
de.summary: Stellt Stepwise vor, ein neuro-symbolisches Beweiserstellungsframework für automatisierte Systemverifikation, das feinabgestimmte LLMs mit interaktiven Theorembeweiser-Tools kombiniert. Der Ansatz erreicht eine Beweisquote von 77,6% beim seL4-Benchmark und übertrifft bisherige LLM-basierte Methoden erheblich.

es.title: Stepwise: Búsqueda de prueba neuro-simbólica para verificación automatizada de sistemas
es.summary: Presenta Stepwise, un marco de generación de pruebas neuro-simbólico para la verificación automatizada de sistemas que combina modelos de lenguaje ajustados con herramientas de demostración de teoremas interactivos. El enfoque logra una tasa de prueba de teorema del 77,6% en el benchmark seL4, superando sustancialmente los métodos anteriores basados en modelos de lenguaje.

title: Embodied Science: Closing the Discovery Loop with Agentic Embodied AI
summary: Proposes embodied science, a paradigm reframing scientific discovery as a closed-loop coupling agentic reasoning with physical execution through the Perception-Language-Action-Discovery framework. This approach bridges computational prediction with empirical validation for autonomous discovery in life and chemical sciences.
content:
arXiv:2603.19782v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Artificial intelligence has demonstrated remarkable capability in predicting scientific properties, yet scientific discovery remains an inherently physical, long-horizon pursuit governed by experimental cycles. Most current computational approaches are misaligned with this reality, framing discovery as isolated, task-specific predictions rather than continuous interaction with the physical world. Here, we argue for embodied science, a paradigm that reframes scientific discovery as a closed loop tightly coupling agentic reasoning with physical execution. We propose a unified Perception-Language-Action-Discovery (PLAD) framework, wherein embodied agents perceive experimental environments, reason over scientific knowledge, execute physical interventions, and internalize outcomes to drive subsequent exploration. By grounding computational reasoning in robust physical feedback, this approach bridges the gap between digital prediction and empirical validation, offering a roadmap for autonomous discovery systems in the life and chemical sciences.

zh.title: 具身科学：通过智能体具身AI关闭发现循环
zh.summary: 提出具身科学，一个范式将科学发现重新框架化为通过感知-语言-行动-发现框架将智能体推理与物理执行耦合的闭环。这种方法在生命和化学科学中桥接计算预测与经验验证，实现自主发现。

fr.title: La Science Incarnée: Fermer la boucle de découverte avec l'IA Incarnée Agentive
fr.summary: Propose la science incarnée, un paradigme reformulant la découverte scientifique comme un couplage de boucle fermée du raisonnement d'agent avec l'exécution physique par le cadre Perception-Langage-Action-Découverte. Cette approche comble la prédiction computationnelle et la validation empirique pour la découverte autonome dans les sciences de la vie et chimiques.

de.title: Embodied Science: Schließung der Entdeckungsschleife mit agentengesteuerter verkörperter KI
de.summary: Schlägt verkörperte Wissenschaft vor, ein Paradigma, das wissenschaftliche Entdeckung als geschlossene Schleife umformuliert, die agentisches Denken mit physischer Ausführung durch das Perception-Language-Action-Discovery-Framework koppelt. Dieser Ansatz verbindet rechnergestützte Vorhersage mit empirischer Validierung für autonome Entdeckung in Lebens- und Chemiewissenschaften.

es.title: Ciencia Encarnada: Cerrando el Bucle de Descubrimiento con IA Encarnada Agentiva
es.summary: Propone la ciencia encarnada, un paradigma que redefine el descubrimiento científico como un acoplamiento de bucle cerrado del razonamiento agentivo con la ejecución física a través del marco Percepción-Lenguaje-Acción-Descubrimiento. Este enfoque cierra la brecha entre predicción computacional y validación empírica para el descubrimiento autónomo en ciencias de la vida y química.

title: FormalEvolve: Neuro-Symbolic Evolutionary Search for Diverse and Prover-Effective Autoformalization
summary: Presents FormalEvolve, a neuro-symbolic evolutionary framework for autoformalization that generates diverse, prover-effective formalizations of natural language mathematics. Using LLM-driven mutation, crossover, and AST rewrites, it achieves 58.0% and 84.9% semantic hit rates on CombiBench and ProofNet respectively.
content:
arXiv:2603.19828v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autoformalization aims to translate natural-language mathematics into compilable, machine-checkable statements. However, semantic consistency does not imply prover effectiveness: even semantically consistent formalizations can differ substantially in proof-search cost and success rate. In this work, we formulate autoformalization as a budgeted, test-time search for semantically consistent repertoires, and propose FormalEvolve, a compilation-gated neuro-symbolic evolutionary framework. FormalEvolve generates diverse candidates via LLM-driven mutation and crossover with bounded patch repair, while symbolic Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) rewrite operations further inject structural diversity. On CombiBench and ProofNet, under a strict generator-call budget of T = 100, FormalEvolve reaches semantic hit rates (SH@100) of 58.0% and 84.9%, and reduces cross-problem concentration of semantic successes(lower Gini). Under a fixed prover budget, FormalEvolve also improves downstream proving performance on CombiBench. Code will be released publicly.

zh.title: FormalEvolve: 用于多样化和证明器有效自动形式化的神经符号进化搜索
zh.summary: 提出FormalEvolve，一个用于自动形式化的神经符号进化框架，生成多样化且对证明器有效的自然语言数学形式化。使用大语言模型驱动的突变、交叉和AST重写，在CombiBench和ProofNet上分别实现了58.0%和84.9%的语义命中率。

fr.title: FormalEvolve: Recherche évolutive neuro-symbolique pour une formalisation automatique diverse et efficace pour le prouveur
fr.summary: Présente FormalEvolve, un cadre évolutif neuro-symbolique pour la formalisation automatique qui génère des formalisations diverses et efficaces du prouveur des mathématiques en langage naturel. Utilisant une mutation pilotée par modèles de langage, un croisement et des réécriture d'AST, elle atteint des taux de réussite sémantique de 58,0% et 84,9% sur CombiBench et ProofNet respectivement.

de.title: FormalEvolve: Neuro-symbolische evolutionäre Suche für vielfältige und beweisereffektive automatische Formalisierung
de.summary: Präsentiert FormalEvolve, ein neuro-symbolisches Evolutionsframework für automatische Formalisierung, das vielfältige, beweisereffektive Formalisierungen natürlichsprachlicher Mathematik generiert. Mit LLM-gesteuerten Mutationen, Crossover und AST-Umschreibungen erreicht es Semantic-Hit-Raten von 58,0% bzw. 84,9% auf CombiBench und ProofNet.

es.title: FormalEvolve: Búsqueda Evolutiva Neuro-Simbólica para Formalización Automática Diversa y Efectiva para el Demostrador
es.summary: Presenta FormalEvolve, un marco evolutivo neuro-simbólico para la formalización automática que genera formalizaciones diversas y efectivas para el demostrador de matemáticas en lenguaje natural. Utilizando mutación, cruzamiento y reescritura de AST impulsados por modelos de lenguaje, logra tasas de acierto semántico del 58,0% y 84,9% en CombiBench y ProofNet respectivamente.

title: Utility-Guided Agent Orchestration for Efficient LLM Tool Use
summary: Proposes a utility-guided orchestration policy for tool-using LLM agents that explicitly balances answer quality and execution cost through selective action decisions. The controllable framework demonstrates that lightweight utility design can reduce tool calls and token consumption while maintaining performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.19896v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Tool-using large language model (LLM) agents often face a fundamental tension between answer quality and execution cost. Fixed workflows are stable but inflexible, while free-form multi-step reasoning methods such as ReAct may improve task performance at the expense of excessive tool calls, longer trajectories, higher token consumption, and increased latency. In this paper, we study agent orchestration as an explicit decision problem rather than leaving it entirely to prompt-level behavior. We propose a utility-guided orchestration policy that selects among actions such as respond, retrieve, tool call, verify, and stop by balancing estimated gain, step cost, uncertainty, and redundancy. Our goal is not to claim universally best task performance, but to provide a controllable and analyzable policy framework for studying quality-cost trade-offs in tool-using LLM agents. Experiments across direct answering, threshold control, fixed workflows, ReAct, and several policy variants show that explicit orchestration signals substantially affect agent behavior. Additional analyses on cost definitions, workflow fairness, and redundancy control further demonstrate that lightweight utility design can provide a defensible and practical mechanism for agent control.

zh.title: 效用引导的代理编排用于高效LLM工具使用
zh.summary: 提出了一种效用引导的编排策略，用于工具使用型LLM代理，通过选择性行动决策明确平衡答案质量和执行成本。可控框架证明轻量级效用设计可以减少工具调用和令牌消耗，同时保持性能。

fr.title: Orchestration guidée par l'utilité pour l'utilisation efficace des outils LLM
fr.summary: Propose une politique d'orchestration guidée par l'utilité pour les agents LLM utilisant des outils qui équilibre explicitement la qualité des réponses et le coût d'exécution par des décisions d'actions sélectives. Le cadre contrôlable démontre que la conception légère des utilitaires peut réduire les appels d'outils et la consommation de tokens tout en maintenant les performances.

de.title: Nutzen-gesteuerte Agentenorchestrierung für effiziente LLM-Werkzeugnutzung
de.summary: Schlägt eine nutzen-gesteuerte Orchestrierungspolitik für werkzeugnutzende LLM-Agenten vor, die explizit die Antwortqualität und Ausführungskosten durch selektive Aktionsentscheidungen ausgleicht. Das kontrollierbare Framework zeigt, dass ein leichtgewichtiges Utility-Design die Anzahl der Werkzeugaufrufe und den Token-Verbrauch reduzieren kann, während die Leistung erhalten bleibt.

es.title: Orquestación guiada por utilidad para el uso eficiente de herramientas LLM
es.summary: Propone una política de orquestación guiada por utilidad para agentes LLM que utilizan herramientas, equilibrando explícitamente la calidad de las respuestas y el costo de ejecución mediante decisiones de acciones selectivas. El marco controlable demuestra que el diseño ligero de utilidades puede reducir las llamadas a herramientas y el consumo de tokens mientras se mantiene el rendimiento.

title: On the Ability of Transformers to Verify Plans
summary: Analyzes when transformers can verify plans in AI planning tasks using C*-RASP, an extension enabling length generalization study under simultaneous growth of sequence length and vocabulary. The work identifies planning domains where transformers provably learn to verify long plans.
content:
arXiv:2603.19954v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Transformers have shown inconsistent success in AI planning tasks, and theoretical understanding of when generalization should be expected has been limited. We take important steps towards addressing this gap by analyzing the ability of decoder-only models to verify whether a given plan correctly solves a given planning instance. To analyse the general setting where the number of objects -- and thus the effective input alphabet -- grows at test time, we introduce C*-RASP, an extension of C-RASP designed to establish length generalization guarantees for transformers under the simultaneous growth in sequence length and vocabulary size. Our results identify a large class of classical planning domains for which transformers can provably learn to verify long plans, and structural properties that significantly affects the learnability of length generalizable solutions. Empirical experiments corroborate our theory.

zh.title: 论变换器验证计划的能力
zh.summary: 使用C*-RASP分析变换器何时能够验证AI规划任务中的计划，C*-RASP是一种扩展，可在序列长度和词汇表同时增长下进行长度泛化研究。该工作确定了变换器可以证明学习验证长计划的规划领域。

fr.title: Sur la capacité des transformateurs à vérifier les plans
fr.summary: Analyse quand les transformateurs peuvent vérifier les plans dans les tâches de planification IA en utilisant C*-RASP, une extension permettant l'étude de la généralisation de longueur sous croissance simultanée de la longueur de séquence et du vocabulaire. Le travail identifie les domaines de planification où les transformateurs apprennent à vérifier les plans longs.

de.title: Über die Fähigkeit von Transformatoren, Pläne zu überprüfen
de.summary: Analysiert, wann Transformer Pläne in KI-Planungsaufgaben überprüfen können, indem C*-RASP verwendet wird, eine Erweiterung, die die Längengeneralisierungsstudie unter gleichzeitigem Wachstum von Sequenzlänge und Vokabular ermöglicht. Die Arbeit identifiziert Planungsdomänen, in denen Transformer nachweislich lernen, lange Pläne zu überprüfen.

es.title: Sobre la capacidad de los transformadores para verificar planes
es.summary: Analiza cuándo los transformadores pueden verificar planes en tareas de planificación de IA utilizando C*-RASP, una extensión que permite estudios de generalización de longitud bajo crecimiento simultáneo de longitud de secuencia y vocabulario. El trabajo identifica dominios de planificación donde los transformadores aprenden comprobadamente a verificar planes largos.

title: Experience is the Best Teacher: Motivating Effective Exploration in Reinforcement Learning for LLMs
summary: Introduces HeRL, a hindsight experience guided reinforcement learning framework that uses failed trajectories as in-context guidance for LLMs to explore desired behaviors. The approach provides more accurate gradient estimation and enables self-improvement at test time across multiple reasoning benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.20046v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) with rubric-based rewards has recently shown remarkable progress in enhancing general reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs), yet still suffers from ineffective exploration confined to curent policy distribution. In fact, RL optimization can be viewed as steering the policy toward an ideal distribution that maximizes the rewards, while effective exploration should align efforts with desired target. Leveraging this insight, we propose HeRL, a Hindsight experience guided Reinforcement Learning framework to bootstrap effective exploration by explicitly telling LLMs the desired behaviors specified in rewards. Concretely, HeRL treats failed trajectories along with their unmet rubrics as hindsight experience, which serves as in-context guidance for the policy to explore desired responses beyond its current distribution. Additionally, we introduce a bonus reward to incentivize responses with greater potential for improvement under such guidance. HeRL facilitates effective learning from desired high quality samples without repeated trial-and-error from scratch, yielding a more accurate estimation of the expected gradient theoretically. Extensive experiments across various benchmarks demonstrate that HeRL achieves superior performance gains over baselines, and can further benefit from experience guided self-improvement at test time. Our code is available at https://github.com/sikelifei/HeRL.

zh.title: 经验是最好的老师：在LLM强化学习中激发有效探索
zh.summary: 介绍了HeRL，一种后见之明经验引导的强化学习框架，利用失败的轨迹作为LLM的上下文指导来探索所需的行为。该方法提供了更准确的梯度估计，并在多个推理基准上实现了测试时的自我改进。

fr.title: L'expérience est le meilleur maître : Motiver une exploration efficace dans l'apprentissage par renforcement pour les LLM
fr.summary: Présente HeRL, un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement guidé par l'expérience rétrospective qui utilise les trajectoires échouées comme guide contextuel pour les LLM afin d'explorer les comportements souhaités. L'approche fournit une estimation de gradient plus précise et permet l'auto-amélioration au moment du test sur plusieurs benchmarks de raisonnement.

de.title: Erfahrung ist der beste Lehrer: Motivierung effektiver Erkundung beim Reinforcement Learning für LLMs
de.summary: Stellt HeRL vor, ein Reinforcement-Learning-Framework mit rückwärtsgewandter Erfahrungsanleitung, das fehlgeschlagene Trajektorien als kontextuelles Leitkonzept für LLMs zur Erkundung gewünschter Verhaltensweisen nutzt. Der Ansatz bietet genauere Gradientenschätzung und ermöglicht Selbstverbesserung zur Testzeit über mehrere Reasoning-Benchmarks hinweg.

es.title: La experiencia es el mejor maestro: Motivar una exploración efectiva en aprendizaje por refuerzo para LLMs
es.summary: Presenta HeRL, un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo guiado por experiencia retrospectiva que utiliza trayectorias fallidas como orientación en contexto para que los LLM exploren comportamientos deseados. El enfoque proporciona una estimación de gradiente más precisa y permite la auto-mejora en tiempo de prueba en múltiples benchmarks de razonamiento.

title: DIAL-KG: Schema-Free Incremental Knowledge Graph Construction via Dynamic Schema Induction and Evolution-Intent Assessment
summary: DIAL-KG is a framework for incrementally building knowledge graphs as new data arrives, eliminating the need for complete reconstruction. The approach dynamically induces schemas from validated knowledge and achieves state-of-the-art performance on both graph construction and schema induction tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.20059v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) are foundational to applications such as search, question answering, and recommendation. Conventional knowledge graph construction methods are predominantly static, rely ing on a single-step construction from a fixed corpus with a prede f
ined schema. However, such methods are suboptimal for real-world sce narios where data arrives dynamically, as incorporating new informa tion requires complete and computationally expensive graph reconstruc tions. Furthermore, predefined schemas hinder the flexibility of knowl edge graph construction. To address these limitations, we introduce DIAL KG, a closed-loop framework for incremental KG construction orches trated by a Meta-Knowledge Base (MKB). The framework oper ates in a three-stage cycle: (i) Dual-Track Extraction, which ensures knowledge completeness by defaulting to triple generation and switching to event extraction for complex knowledge; (ii) Governance Adjudica tion, which ensures the fidelity and currency of extracted facts to prevent hallucinations and knowledge staleness; and (iii) Schema Evolution, in which new schemas are induced from validated knowledge to guide subsequent construction cycles, and knowledge from the current round is incrementally applied to the existing KG. Extensive experiments demon strate that our framework achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in the quality of both the constructed graph and the induced schemas.

zh.title: DIAL-KG：通过动态模式诱导和演变意图评估的无模式增量知识图谱构建
zh.summary: DIAL-KG是一个框架，用于在新数据到达时逐步构建知识图，消除了完整重建的需要。该方法从验证知识中动态诱导模式，并在图构建和模式诱导任务中都实现了最先进的性能。

fr.title: DIAL-KG : Construction incrémentale de graphe de connaissances sans schéma via induction de schéma dynamique et évaluation d'intention d'évolution
fr.summary: DIAL-KG est un cadre pour construire progressivement des graphes de connaissances à l'arrivée de nouvelles données, éliminant le besoin de reconstruction complète. L'approche induit dynamiquement des schémas à partir de connaissances validées et atteint les meilleures performances sur les tâches de construction de graphe et d'induction de schéma.

de.title: DIAL-KG: Schemafreier inkrementeller Wissensdiagrammaufbau durch dynamische Schemainduktion und Evolutionsabsichtsbewertung
de.summary: DIAL-KG ist ein Framework für die inkrementelle Erstellung von Wissensgraphen bei Ankunft neuer Daten, wodurch die Notwendigkeit einer vollständigen Rekonstruktion entfällt. Der Ansatz induziert dynamisch Schemata aus validiertem Wissen und erreicht modernste Leistung bei Graphenkonstruktions- und Schemainduktionsaufgaben.

es.title: DIAL-KG: Construcción incremental de gráficos de conocimiento sin esquema mediante inducción de esquema dinámico y evaluación de intención de evolución
es.summary: DIAL-KG es un marco para construir gráficos de conocimiento incrementalmente a medida que llegan nuevos datos, eliminando la necesidad de reconstrucción completa. El enfoque induce dinámicamente esquemas a partir de conocimiento validado y logra un rendimiento de vanguardia en las tareas de construcción de gráficos e inducción de esquemas.

title: Pitfalls in Evaluating Interpretability Agents
summary: Study reveals fundamental challenges in evaluating automated interpretability systems, showing that replication-based evaluation can miss important failure modes. Researchers propose unsupervised intrinsic evaluation methods to address limitations in assessing complex AI interpretability agents.
content:
arXiv:2603.20101v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Automated interpretability systems aim to reduce the need for human labor and scale analysis to increasingly large models and diverse tasks. Recent efforts toward this goal leverage large language models (LLMs) at increasing levels of autonomy, ranging from fixed one-shot workflows to fully autonomous interpretability agents. This shift creates a corresponding need to scale evaluation approaches to keep pace with both the volume and complexity of generated explanations. We investigate this challenge in the context of automated circuit analysis -- explaining the roles of model components when performing specific tasks. To this end, we build an agentic system in which a research agent iteratively designs experiments and refines hypotheses. When evaluated against human expert explanations across six circuit analysis tasks in the literature, the system appears competitive. However, closer examination reveals several pitfalls of replication-based evaluation: human expert explanations can be subjective or incomplete, outcome-based comparisons obscure the research process, and LLM-based systems may reproduce published findings via memorization or informed guessing. To address some of these pitfalls, we propose an unsupervised intrinsic evaluation based on the functional interchangeability of model components. Our work demonstrates fundamental challenges in evaluating complex automated interpretability systems and reveals key limitations of replication-based evaluation.

zh.title: 评估可解释性代理的陷阱
zh.summary: 研究揭示了评估自动化可解释性系统的根本性挑战，表明基于复制的评估可能会遗漏重要的失败模式。研究人员提出了无监督内在评估方法来解决评估复杂AI可解释性代理的局限性。

fr.title: Pièges dans l'évaluation des agents d'interprétabilité
fr.summary: L'étude révèle des défis fondamentaux dans l'évaluation des systèmes d'interprétabilité automatisés, montrant que l'évaluation basée sur la réplication peut manquer les modes de défaillance importants. Les chercheurs proposent des méthodes d'évaluation intrinsèque non supervisées pour remédier aux limitations de l'évaluation des agents d'interprétabilité IA complexes.

de.title: Fallstricke bei der Bewertung von Interpretierungsagenten
de.summary: Die Studie offenbart grundlegende Herausforderungen bei der Bewertung automatisierter Interpretabilitätssysteme und zeigt, dass die replikationsbasierte Bewertung wichtige Fehlermodi übersehen kann. Forscher schlagen unbeaufsichtigte intrinsische Bewertungsmethoden vor, um Einschränkungen bei der Bewertung komplexer KI-Interpretabilitätsagenten zu beheben.

es.title: Trampas en la evaluación de agentes de interpretabilidad
es.summary: El estudio revela desafíos fundamentales en la evaluación de sistemas de interpretabilidad automatizados, mostrando que la evaluación basada en replicación puede perder modos de fallo importantes. Los investigadores proponen métodos de evaluación intrínseca no supervisada para abordar las limitaciones en la evaluación de agentes complejos de interpretabilidad de IA.

title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs for Theory-of-mind Reasoning
summary: Researchers propose dynamic belief graphs to improve theory-of-mind reasoning in LLMs for high-stakes scenarios like disaster response. The structured approach better captures how beliefs evolve and influence decisions, producing more coherent mental state representations than prior methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.20170v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Theory of Mind (ToM) reasoning with Large Language Models (LLMs) requires inferring how people's implicit, evolving beliefs shape what they seek and how they act under uncertainty -- especially in high-stakes settings such as disaster response, emergency medicine, and human-in-the-loop autonomy. Prior approaches either prompt LLMs directly or use latent-state models that treat beliefs as static and independent, often producing incoherent mental models over time and weak reasoning in dynamic contexts. We introduce a structured cognitive trajectory model for LLM-based ToM that represents mental state as a dynamic belief graph, jointly inferring latent beliefs, learning their time-varying dependencies, and linking belief evolution to information seeking and decisions. Our model contributes (i) a novel projection from textualized probabilistic statements to consistent probabilistic graphical model updates, (ii) an energy-based factor graph representation of belief interdependencies, and (iii) an ELBO-based objective that captures belief accumulation and delayed decisions. Across multiple real-world disaster evacuation datasets, our model significantly improves action prediction and recovers interpretable belief trajectories consistent with human reasoning, providing a principled module for augmenting LLMs with ToM in high-uncertainty environment. https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ICML_submission-6373/

zh.title: 学习动态信念图用于心智理论推理
zh.summary: 研究人员提出动态信念图来改进大语言模型在灾难应对等高风险场景中的心智理论推理。这种结构化方法能更好地捕捉信念如何演变和影响决策，相比先前的方法产生更连贯的心智状态表示。

fr.title: Apprentissage de Graphes de Croyances Dynamiques pour le Raisonnement de Théorie de l'Esprit
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent des graphes de croyances dynamiques pour améliorer le raisonnement de théorie de l'esprit dans les LLM pour des scénarios à enjeux élevés comme la réponse aux catastrophes. L'approche structurée capture mieux comment les croyances évoluent et influencent les décisions, produisant des représentations d'états mentaux plus cohérentes que les méthodes précédentes.

de.title: Lernen dynamischer Glaubensnetze für das Reasoning von Theory-of-Mind
de.summary: Forscher schlagen dynamische Glaubensnetze vor, um das Theory-of-Mind-Reasoning in LLMs für hochrisikante Szenarien wie Katastrophenreaktion zu verbessern. Der strukturierte Ansatz erfasst besser, wie sich Überzeugungen entwickeln und Entscheidungen beeinflussen, und erzeugt kohärentere mentale Zustandsrepräsentationen als frühere Methoden.

es.title: Aprendizaje de Gráficos de Creencias Dinámicas para el Razonamiento de Teoría de la Mente
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen gráficos de creencias dinámicas para mejorar el razonamiento de teoría de la mente en LLMs para escenarios de alto riesgo como la respuesta a desastres. El enfoque estructurado captura mejor cómo las creencias evolucionan e influyen en las decisiones, produciendo representaciones de estados mentales más coherentes que los métodos anteriores.

title: Grounded Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Drafting of Radiology Impressions Using Case-Based Similarity Search
summary: A multimodal retrieval-augmented generation system combines image and text embeddings to retrieve similar chest radiographs for grounding radiology report generation. The approach reduces hallucinations and provides explicit citation traceability, improving trustworthiness compared to fully generative alternatives.
content:
arXiv:2603.17765v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Automated radiology report generation has gained increasing attention with the rise of deep learning and large language models. However, fully generative approaches often suffer from hallucinations and lack clinical grounding, limiting their reliability in real-world workflows. In this study, we propose a multimodal retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system for grounded drafting of chest radiograph impressions. The system combines contrastive image-text embeddings, case-based similarity retrieval, and citation-constrained draft generation to ensure factual alignment with historical radiology reports. A curated subset of the MIMIC-CXR dataset was used to construct a multimodal retrieval database. Image embeddings were generated using CLIP encoders, while textual embeddings were derived from structured impression sections. A fusion similarity framework was implemented using FAISS indexing for scalable nearest-neighbor retrieval. Retrieved cases were used to construct grounded prompts for draft impression generation, with safety mechanisms enforcing citation coverage and confidence-based refusal. Experimental results demonstrate that multimodal fusion significantly improves retrieval performance compared to image-only retrieval, achieving Recall@5 above 0.95 on clinically relevant findings. The grounded drafting pipeline produces interpretable outputs with explicit citation traceability, enabling improved trustworthiness compared to conventional generative approaches. This work highlights the potential of retrieval-augmented multimodal systems for reliable clinical decision support and radiology workflow augmentation

zh.title: 基于案例相似性搜索的接地多模态检索增强放射学印象草拟
zh.summary: 一个多模态检索增强生成系统结合图像和文本嵌入来检索相似的胸部X光片以支撑放射学报告生成。该方法减少了幻觉并提供了明确的引用可追溯性，相比完全生成性的替代方案提高了可信度。

fr.title: Génération Ancrée Multimodale Augmentée par Récupération d'Impressions Radiologiques Utilisant une Recherche de Similarité Basée sur les Cas
fr.summary: Un système de génération augmentée par récupération multimodale combine les plongements d'images et de texte pour récupérer des radiographies thoraciques similaires pour l'ancrage de la génération de rapports radiologiques. L'approche réduit les hallucinations et fournit une traçabilité des citations explicites, améliorant la fiabilité par rapport aux alternatives entièrement génératives.

de.title: Verankerte Multimodale Abruf-Augmentierte Erstellung von Radiologie-Impressionen unter Verwendung von Fallbasierten Ähnlichkeitssuchen
de.summary: Ein multimodales abrufgesteuertes Generierungssystem kombiniert Bild- und Texteinbettungen, um ähnliche Brustaufnahmen zum Verankern der Radiologieberichtgenerierung zu finden. Der Ansatz reduziert Halluzinationen und bietet explizite Zitierbarkeit, was die Vertrauenswürdigkeit im Vergleich zu rein generativen Alternativen verbessert.

es.title: Generación Anclada Multimodal Aumentada por Recuperación de Impresiones Radiológicas Utilizando Búsqueda de Similitud Basada en Casos
es.summary: Un sistema de generación aumentada por recuperación multimodal combina incrustaciones de imágenes y texto para recuperar radiografías de tórax similares para fundamentar la generación de informes radiológicos. El enfoque reduce alucinaciones y proporciona trazabilidad explícita de citas, mejorando la confiabilidad en comparación con alternativas puramente generativas.

title: L-PRISMA: An Extension of PRISMA in the Era of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI)
summary: Researchers extend the PRISMA systematic review framework to responsibly incorporate generative AI while preserving reproducibility and transparency. The approach combines human oversight with AI-assisted pre-screening and deterministic statistical methods to enable reliable GenAI integration in evidence synthesis.
content:
arXiv:2603.19236v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) framework provides a rigorous foundation for evidence synthesis, yet the manual processes of data extraction and literature screening remain time-consuming and restrictive. Recent advances in Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI), particularly large language models (LLMs), offer opportunities to automate and scale these tasks, thereby improving time and efficiency. However, reproducibility, transparency, and auditability, the core PRISMA principles, are being challenged by the inherent non-determinism of LLMs and the risks of hallucination and bias amplification. To address these limitations, this study integrates human-led synthesis with a GenAI-assisted statistical pre-screening step. Human oversight ensures scientific validity and transparency, while the deterministic nature of the statistical layer enhances reproducibility. The proposed approach systematically enhances PRISMA guidelines, providing a responsible pathway for incorporating GenAI into systematic review workflows.

zh.title: L-PRISMA：生成式人工智能时代PRISMA的扩展
zh.summary: 研究人员扩展了PRISMA系统评审框架，以负责任地整合生成式人工智能，同时保持可重复性和透明性。该方法将人类监督与AI辅助的预筛选和确定性统计方法相结合，以实现证据合成中可靠的生成式人工智能整合。

fr.title: L-PRISMA : Une Extension de PRISMA à l'Ère de l'Intelligence Artificielle Générative (GenAI)
fr.summary: Les chercheurs étendent le cadre d'examen systématique PRISMA pour intégrer de manière responsable l'IA générative tout en préservant la reproductibilité et la transparence. L'approche combine la surveillance humaine avec le pré-criblage assisté par l'IA et les méthodes statistiques déterministes pour permettre une intégration fiable de la GenAI dans la synthèse des données probantes.

de.title: L-PRISMA: Eine Erweiterung von PRISMA im Zeitalter der Generativen Künstlichen Intelligenz (GenAI)
de.summary: Forscher erweitern das PRISMA-Rahmenwerk für systematische Überprüfungen, um KI generativ verantwortungsvoll zu integrieren und gleichzeitig Reproduzierbarkeit und Transparenz zu bewahren. Der Ansatz kombiniert menschliche Aufsicht mit KI-gestütztem Vor-Screening und deterministischen statistischen Methoden, um eine zuverlässige GenAI-Integration in der Evidenzsynthese zu ermöglichen.

es.title: L-PRISMA: Una Extensión de PRISMA en la Era de la Inteligencia Artificial Generativa (GenAI)
es.summary: Los investigadores extienden el marco de revisión sistemática PRISMA para integrar responsablemente la IA generativa mientras preservan la reproducibilidad y la transparencia. El enfoque combina la supervisión humana con el pre-cribado asistido por IA y métodos estadísticos deterministas para permitir la integración confiable de GenAI en la síntesis de evidencia.

title: When Prompt Optimization Becomes Jailbreaking: Adaptive Red-Teaming of Large Language Models
summary: Study demonstrates that LLMs are vulnerable to automated adversarial prompt refinement techniques originally designed for benign optimization. Results show significant reductions in safety safeguards, particularly for smaller open-source models, suggesting static benchmarks underestimate security risks.
content:
arXiv:2603.19247v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into high-stakes applications, making robust safety guarantees a central practical and commercial concern. Existing safety evaluations predominantly rely on fixed collections of harmful prompts, implicitly assuming non-adaptive adversaries and thereby overlooking realistic attack scenarios in which inputs are iteratively refined to evade safeguards. In this work, we examine the vulnerability of contemporary language models to automated, adversarial prompt refinement. We repurpose black-box prompt optimization techniques, originally designed to improve performance on benign tasks, to systematically search for safety failures. Using DSPy, we apply three such optimizers to prompts drawn from HarmfulQA and JailbreakBench, explicitly optimizing toward a continuous danger score in the range 0 to 1 provided by an independent evaluator model (GPT-5.1). Our results demonstrate a substantial reduction in effective safety safeguards, with the effects being especially pronounced for open-source small language models. For example, the average danger score of Qwen 3 8B increases from 0.09 in its baseline setting to 0.79 after optimization. These findings suggest that static benchmarks may underestimate residual risk, indicating that automated, adaptive red-teaming is a necessary component of robust safety evaluation.

zh.title: 当提示优化成为越狱：大型语言模型的自适应红队测试
zh.summary: 研究表明，大型语言模型容易受到原本为良性优化设计的自动化对抗性提示优化技术的攻击。结果显示安全保护措施显著下降，特别是对于较小的开源模型，这表明静态基准低估了安全风险。

fr.title: Quand l'Optimisation des Invites Devient du Jailbreaking : Test Adversaire Adaptatif des Grands Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: L'étude démontre que les LLM sont vulnérables aux techniques de raffinement de messages contradictoires automatisées originellement conçues pour l'optimisation bénigne. Les résultats montrent des réductions significatives des garde-fous de sécurité, particulièrement pour les modèles open-source plus petits, suggérant que les benchmarks statiques sous-estiment les risques de sécurité.

de.title: Wenn Prompt-Optimierung zu Jailbreaking wird: Adaptive Red-Teaming großer Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Eine Studie zeigt, dass große Sprachmodelle anfällig für automatisierte gegnerische Prompt-Verfeinerungstechniken sind, die ursprünglich für gutartige Optimierung entwickelt wurden. Die Ergebnisse zeigen signifikante Reduzierungen von Sicherheitsvorkehrungen, besonders bei kleineren Open-Source-Modellen, was darauf hindeutet, dass statische Benchmarks Sicherheitsrisiken unterschätzen.

es.title: Cuando la Optimización de Indicaciones se Convierte en Jailbreaking: Pruebas Adversarias Adaptativas de Modelos de Lenguaje Grande
es.summary: Un estudio demuestra que los LLM son vulnerables a técnicas de refinamiento de indicaciones adversarias automatizadas diseñadas originalmente para optimización benigna. Los resultados muestran reducciones significativas de salvaguardas de seguridad, particularmente para modelos de código abierto más pequeños, sugiriendo que los puntos de referencia estáticos subestiman los riesgos de seguridad.

title: DuCCAE: A Hybrid Engine for Immersive Conversation via Collaboration, Augmentation, and Evolution
summary: Baidu deployed DuCCAE, a hybrid conversational system that decouples real-time response generation from asynchronous agentic task execution while maintaining dialogue coherence. Production metrics since June 2025 show tripled user retention and 65.2% complex task completion rate.
content:
arXiv:2603.19248v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Immersive conversational systems in production face a persistent trade-off between responsiveness and long-horizon task capability. Real-time interaction is achievable for lightweight turns, but requests involving planning and tool invocation (e.g., search and media generation) produce heavy-tail execution latency that degrades turn-taking, persona consistency, and user trust. To address this challenge, we propose DuCCAE (Conversation while Collaboration with Augmentation and Evolution), a hybrid engine for immersive conversation deployed within Baidu Search, serving millions of users. DuCCAE decouples real-time response generation from asynchronous agentic execution and synchronizes them via a shared state that maintains session context and execution traces, enabling asynchronous results to be integrated back into the ongoing dialogue. The system orchestrates five subsystems-Info, Conversation, Collaboration, Augmentation, and Evolution-to support multi-agent collaboration and continuous improvement. We evaluate DuCCAE through a comprehensive framework that combines offline benchmarking on the Du-Interact dataset and large-scale production evaluation within Baidu Search. Experimental results demonstrate that DuCCAE outperforms strong baselines in agentic execution reliability and dialogue quality while reducing latency to fit strict real-time budgets. Crucially, deployment metrics since June 2025 confirm substantial real-world effectiveness, evidenced by a tripling of Day-7 user retention to 34.2% and a surge in the complex task completion rate to 65.2%. Our hybrid architecture successfully preserves conversational continuity while enabling reliable agentic execution, offering practical guidelines for deploying scalable agentic systems in industrial settings.

zh.title: DuCCAE：通过协作、增强和演进实现沉浸式对话的混合引擎
zh.summary: 百度部署了DuCCAE，一个混合会话系统，可以将实时响应生成与异步代理任务执行分离，同时保持对话的连贯性。自2025年6月以来的生产指标显示用户留存率提高了三倍，复杂任务完成率达到65.2%。

fr.title: DuCCAE : Un Moteur Hybride pour une Conversation Immersive via la Collaboration, l'Augmentation et l'Évolution
fr.summary: Baidu a déployé DuCCAE, un système conversationnel hybride qui découple la génération de réponses en temps réel de l'exécution asynchrone de tâches d'agent tout en maintenant la cohérence du dialogue. Les métriques de production depuis juin 2025 montrent une rétention utilisateur triplée et un taux de complétion de tâches complexes de 65,2%.

de.title: DuCCAE: Ein Hybrid-Engine für immersive Konversation durch Zusammenarbeit, Erweiterung und Entwicklung
