de.summary: Baidu hat DuCCAE eingeführt, ein hybrides Gesprächssystem, das die Generierung von Echtzeitantworten von der asynchronen Ausführung von Agent-Aufgaben trennt und gleichzeitig die Dialogkohärenz bewahrt. Produktionsmetriken seit Juni 2025 zeigen eine verdreifachte Benutzerbindung und eine Quote zur Fertigstellung komplexer Aufgaben von 65,2%.

es.title: DuCCAE: Un Motor Híbrido para Conversación Inmersiva a través de Colaboración, Aumento y Evolución
es.summary: Baidu implementó DuCCAE, un sistema conversacional híbrido que desvincula la generación de respuestas en tiempo real de la ejecución asincrónica de tareas de agentes mientras mantiene la coherencia del diálogo. Las métricas de producción desde junio de 2025 muestran una retención de usuarios triplicada y una tasa de finalización de tareas complejas del 65.2%.

title: GeoChallenge: A Multi-Answer Multiple-Choice Benchmark for Geometric Reasoning with Diagrams
summary: GeoChallenge is a 90K-problem benchmark requiring LLMs to perform multi-step geometric reasoning with both diagrams and textual descriptions. Analysis reveals significant performance gaps between models and humans, and identifies systematic failure patterns including weak visual reliance and reasoning without convergence.
content:
arXiv:2603.19252v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Evaluating the symbolic reasoning of large language models (LLMs) calls for geometry benchmarks that require multi-step proofs grounded in both text and diagrams. However, existing benchmarks are often limited in scale and rarely provide visually grounded multiple-choice questions, limiting reliable evaluation of complex reasoning. We introduce GeoChallenge, a dataset of 90K automatically generated multiple-choice geometry proof problems, each requiring multi-step reasoning over aligned textual descriptions and diagrams. GeoChallenge provides fine-grained complexity ratings and formal language annotations to enable controlled evaluation.
  Experiments on multiple advanced LLMs show a clear performance gap between models and humans (the best-performing model, GPT-5-nano, achieves 75.89 exact match vs. 94.74 for humans). Further analysis also reveals three common failure patterns of LLMs: (1) exact match failures under the multiple-choice setting; (2) weak visual reliance; and (3) overextended reasoning without convergence.

zh.title: GeoChallenge：几何推理多答案选择题基准
zh.summary: GeoChallenge是一个包含90K个问题的基准，要求大型语言模型进行多步几何推理，涉及图表和文本描述。分析表明模型和人类之间存在显著的性能差异，并识别了系统性的失败模式，包括对视觉的依赖性弱和没有收敛的推理。

fr.title: GeoChallenge : Un Benchmark Multi-Réponses à Choix Multiples pour le Raisonnement Géométrique avec Diagrammes
fr.summary: GeoChallenge est un benchmark de 90K problèmes nécessitant que les LLM effectuent un raisonnement géométrique multi-étapes avec des diagrammes et des descriptions textuelles. L'analyse révèle des écarts de performance significatifs entre les modèles et les humains, et identifie des schémas d'échec systématiques incluant une faible dépendance visuelle et un raisonnement sans convergence.

de.title: GeoChallenge: Ein Multi-Answer Multiple-Choice-Benchmark für geometrisches Denken mit Diagrammen
de.summary: GeoChallenge ist ein Benchmark mit 90.000 Problemen, die LLMs erfordern, mehrschrittiges geometrisches Denken mit Diagrammen und Textbeschreibungen durchzuführen. Die Analyse zeigt erhebliche Leistungsunterschiede zwischen Modellen und Menschen und identifiziert systematische Fehlermuster, einschließlich schwacher visueller Abhängigkeit und Denken ohne Konvergenz.

es.title: GeoChallenge: Un Benchmark Multi-respuesta de Opción Múltiple para Razonamiento Geométrico con Diagramas
es.summary: GeoChallenge es un benchmark de 90K problemas que requiere que los LLM realicen razonamiento geométrico de múltiples pasos con diagramas y descripciones textuales. El análisis revela brechas de rendimiento significativas entre modelos y humanos, e identifica patrones de falla sistemática incluyendo débil dependencia visual y razonamiento sin convergencia.

title: A comprehensive study of LLM-based argument classification: from Llama through DeepSeek to GPT-5.2
summary: Comprehensive evaluation of state-of-the-art LLMs on argument mining shows GPT-5.2 achieves up to 91.9% accuracy using advanced prompting strategies. Qualitative analysis reveals systematic failure modes shared across models, including difficulties with implicit arguments and complex argument structures.
content:
arXiv:2603.19253v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Argument mining (AM) is an interdisciplinary research field focused on the automatic identification and classification of argumentative components, such as claims and premises, and the relationships between them. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have significantly improved the performance of argument classification compared to traditional machine learning approaches. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of several state-of-the-art LLMs, including GPT-5.2, Llama 4, and DeepSeek, on large publicly available argument classification corpora such as Args.me and UKP. The evaluation incorporates advanced prompting strategies, including Chain-of- Thought prompting, prompt rephrasing, voting, and certainty-based classification. Both quantitative performance metrics and qualitative error analysis are conducted to assess model behavior. The best-performing model in the study (GPT-5.2) achieves a classification accuracy of 78.0% (UKP) and 91.9% (Args.me). The use of prompt rephrasing, multi-prompt voting, and certainty estimation further improves classification performance and robustness. These techniques increase the accuracy and F1 metric of the models by typically a few percentage points (from 2% to 8%). However, qualitative analysis reveals systematic failure modes shared across models, including instabilities with respect to prompt formulation, difficulties in detecting implicit criticism, interpreting complex argument structures, and aligning arguments with specific claims. This work contributes the first comprehensive evaluation that combines quantitative benchmarking and qualitative error analysis on multiple argument mining datasets using advanced LLM prompting strategies.

zh.title: 基于LLM的论证分类综合研究：从Llama到DeepSeek再到GPT-5.2
zh.summary: 对最先进的LLM在论证挖掘方面的综合评估显示，GPT-5.2使用高级提示策略可达到91.9%的准确率。定性分析揭示了模型间共享的系统性失败模式，包括对隐含论证和复杂论证结构的处理困难。

fr.title: Une étude complète de la classification d'arguments basée sur LLM : de Llama via DeepSeek à GPT-5.2
fr.summary: Une évaluation complète des LLM de pointe sur l'extraction d'arguments montre que GPT-5.2 atteint une précision jusqu'à 91,9% en utilisant des stratégies d'invite avancées. L'analyse qualitative révèle des modes de défaillance systématiques partagés entre les modèles, y compris des difficultés avec les arguments implicites et les structures d'arguments complexes.

de.title: Eine umfassende Studie zur LLM-gestützten Argumentklassifizierung: von Llama über DeepSeek bis GPT-5.2
de.summary: Eine umfassende Bewertung modernster LLMs beim Argumentabbau zeigt, dass GPT-5.2 eine Genauigkeit von bis zu 91,9% erreicht, indem erweiterte Prompt-Strategien verwendet werden. Die qualitative Analyse offenbart systematische Fehlermodi, die Modelle gemeinsam aufweisen, einschließlich Schwierigkeiten mit impliziten Argumenten und komplexen Argumentstrukturen.

es.title: Un estudio completo de la clasificación de argumentos basada en LLM: de Llama a través de DeepSeek a GPT-5.2
es.summary: Una evaluación integral de los LLM de última generación en minería de argumentos muestra que GPT-5.2 logra una precisión de hasta el 91,9% utilizando estrategias de indicación avanzadas. El análisis cualitativo revela modos de falla sistemáticos compartidos entre modelos, incluidas dificultades con argumentos implícitos y estructuras de argumentos complejas.

title: LARFT: Closing the Cognition-Action Gap for Length Instruction Following in Large Language Models
summary: LARFT is a training framework that improves LLMs' ability to follow precise length instructions by aligning length cognition with actions through reinforcement learning. The approach achieves 20.92-point average improvement on length instruction benchmarks with minimal decline on general model capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.19255v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite the strong performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) on complex instruction-following tasks, precise control of output length remains a persistent challenge. Existing methods primarily attempt to enforce length constraints by externally imposing length signals or optimization objectives, while largely overlooking the underlying limitation: the model's intrinsic deficit in length cognition. To address this, we propose LARFT (Length-Aware Reinforcement Fine-Tuning), a training framework that aligns the model's length cognition with its action. Specifically, LARFT integrates length-oriented reinforcement learning with a hindsight length awareness. By transforming on-policy data into hindsight self-awareness tasks where the model learns to identify the actual length of its own generation, LARFT jointly optimizes the model's internal representation of length information and refines its policy to satisfy length constraints, thereby achieving precise and reliable length instruction following. Extensive experiments across four base models demonstrate that LARFT outperforms existing baselines, achieving an average improvement of +20.92 points across three length instruction following benchmarks with only a marginal decline of -1.45 points on four general capability benchmarks.

zh.title: LARFT：为大型语言模型中的长度指令遵循关闭认知-行动差距
zh.summary: LARFT是一个训练框架，通过强化学习将长度认知与行动对齐，提高了LLM遵循精确长度指令的能力。该方法在长度指令基准上实现了20.92分的平均改进，同时对通用模型能力的下降最小。

fr.title: LARFT : Combler l'écart cognition-action pour le suivi des instructions de longueur dans les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: LARFT est un cadre d'entraînement qui améliore la capacité des LLMs à suivre les instructions de longueur précises en alignant la cognition de longueur avec les actions par apprentissage par renforcement. L'approche atteint une amélioration moyenne de 20,92 points sur les benchmarks d'instructions de longueur avec un déclin minimal des capacités générales du modèle.

de.title: LARFT: Schließung der Kognitions-Aktions-Lücke beim Befolgen von Längeinstruktionen in großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: LARFT ist ein Trainingsrahmen, der die Fähigkeit von LLMs verbessert, genaue Längeinstruktionen zu befolgen, indem Längenkognition durch Verstärkungslernen mit Aktionen abgestimmt wird. Der Ansatz erreicht eine durchschnittliche Verbesserung von 20,92 Punkten bei Längeinstruktions-Benchmarks mit minimalem Rückgang der allgemeinen Modellkapazitäten.

es.title: LARFT: Cerrando la brecha cognición-acción para seguir instrucciones de longitud en modelos de lenguaje grandes
es.summary: LARFT es un marco de entrenamiento que mejora la capacidad de los LLM para seguir instrucciones de longitud precisas alineando la cognición de longitud con las acciones a través del aprendizaje por refuerzo. El enfoque logra una mejora promedio de 20,92 puntos en puntos de referencia de instrucciones de longitud con un declive mínimo en las capacidades generales del modelo.

title: MAPLE: Metadata Augmented Private Language Evolution
summary: MAPLE addresses differentially private synthetic data generation when foundation models are only accessible via APIs. The framework uses private metadata extraction and in-context learning to improve convergence, utility, and API cost efficiency compared to previous privacy-preserving approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.19258v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While differentially private (DP) fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs) is a powerful tool, it is often computationally prohibitive or infeasible when state-of-the-art models are only accessible via proprietary APIs. In such settings, generating DP synthetic data has emerged as a crucial alternative, offering the added benefits of arbitrary reuse across downstream tasks and transparent exploratory data analysis without the opaque constraints of a model's parameter space. Private Evolution (PE) is a promising API-based framework for this goal; however, its performance critically depends on initialization. When the private data distribution deviates substantially from the foundation model's pre-training priors--particularly in highly specialized domains--PE frequently struggles to align with the target data, resulting in degraded utility, poor convergence, and inefficient API usage. To address this initialization bottleneck, we propose Metadata Augmented Private Language Evolution (MAPLE). MAPLE leverages differentially private tabular metadata extraction and in-context learning to effectively ground the initial synthetic distribution in the target domain. Extensive experiments on challenging, domain-specific text generation tasks demonstrate that MAPLE achieves a significantly more favorable privacy-utility trade-off, converges faster, and drastically reduces API costs compared to previous PE methods.

zh.title: MAPLE：元数据增强的私有语言演化
zh.summary: MAPLE解决了当基础模型仅通过API可访问时的差分隐私合成数据生成问题。该框架使用私有元数据提取和上下文学习来改进收敛性、实用性和API成本效率，与以前的隐私保护方法相比有改进。

fr.title: MAPLE : Évolution du langage privé augmentée par les métadonnées
fr.summary: MAPLE aborde la génération de données synthétiques différentiellement privées lorsque les modèles de base ne sont accessibles que via des API. Le cadre utilise l'extraction de métadonnées privées et l'apprentissage en contexte pour améliorer la convergence, l'utilité et l'efficacité des coûts des API par rapport aux approches précédentes de préservation de la vie privée.

de.title: MAPLE: Metadaten-erweiterte private Sprachentwicklung
de.summary: MAPLE befasst sich mit differenziell privater synthetischer Datenerzeugung, wenn Grundmodelle nur über APIs zugänglich sind. Das Framework verwendet private Metadatenextraktion und In-Context-Learning, um Konvergenz, Dienstprogramm und API-Kosteneffizienz im Vergleich zu früheren datenschutzerhaltenden Ansätzen zu verbessern.

es.title: MAPLE: Evolución del lenguaje privado aumentada con metadatos
es.summary: MAPLE aborda la generación de datos sintéticos diferencialmente privados cuando los modelos de fundación solo son accesibles a través de APIs. El marco utiliza extracción de metadatos privados y aprendizaje en contexto para mejorar la convergencia, utilidad y eficiencia de costos de API en comparación con enfoques anteriores que preservan la privacidad.

title: Breeze Taigi: Benchmarks and Models for Taiwanese Hokkien Speech Recognition and Synthesis
summary: Breeze Taigi introduces comprehensive benchmarks for evaluating Taiwanese Hokkien speech recognition and synthesis systems. The researchers' Whisper-based ASR model achieves 30.13% character error rate, outperforming existing commercial and research systems on the benchmark.
content:
arXiv:2603.19259v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Taiwanese Hokkien (Taigi) presents unique opportunities for advancing speech technology methodologies that can generalize to diverse linguistic contexts. We introduce Breeze Taigi, a comprehensive framework centered on standardized benchmarks for evaluating Taigi speech recognition and synthesis systems. Our primary contribution is a reproducible evaluation methodology that leverages parallel Taiwanese Mandarin resources. We provide 30 carefully curated Mandarin-Taigi audio pairs from Taiwan's Executive Yuan public service announcements with normalized ground truth transcriptions. We establish Character Error Rate (CER) as the standard metric and implement normalization procedures to enable fair cross-system comparisons. To demonstrate the benchmark's utility and provide reference implementations, we develop speech recognition and synthesis models through a methodology that leverages existing Taiwanese Mandarin resources and large-scale synthetic data generation. In particular, we fine-tune a Whisper model on approximately 10,000 hours of Taigi synthetic speech data. Our ASR model achieves 30.13% average CER on the benchmark, outperforming existing commercial and research systems. By providing standardized evaluation protocols, diverse training datasets, and open baseline models, we offer a replicable framework with methodologies applicable to various linguistic contexts.

zh.title: 微風台語：臺灣閩南語語音辨識與合成的基準和模型
zh.summary: Breeze Taigi引入了用於評估臺灣閩南語語音辨識和合成系統的綜合基準。研究人員基於Whisper的自動語音識別模型在基準上實現了30.13%的字符錯誤率，優於現有的商業和研究系統。

fr.title: Breeze Taigi : Critères de référence et modèles pour la reconnaissance et la synthèse vocales du hokkien taïwanais
fr.summary: Breeze Taigi introduit des critères de référence complets pour évaluer les systèmes de reconnaissance et de synthèse vocales du hokkien taïwanais. Le modèle ASR basé sur Whisper des chercheurs atteint un taux d'erreur caractéristique de 30,13 %, surpassant les systèmes commerciaux et de recherche existants.

de.title: Breeze Taigi: Benchmarks und Modelle für taiwanesische Hokkien-Spracherkennung und -Synthese
de.summary: Breeze Taigi führt umfassende Benchmarks zur Bewertung von Spracherkennungs- und Synthesesystemen des taiwanesischen Hokkien ein. Das Whisper-basierte ASR-Modell der Forscher erreicht eine Zeichenfehlerrate von 30,13 %, übertrifft bestehende kommerzielle und Forschungssysteme beim Benchmark.

es.title: Breeze Taigi: Puntos de referencia y modelos para reconocimiento y síntesis de voz Hokkien taiwanés
es.summary: Breeze Taigi introduce puntos de referencia integrales para evaluar sistemas de reconocimiento y síntesis de voz Hokkien taiwanés. El modelo ASR basado en Whisper de los investigadores logra una tasa de error de caracteres del 30,13%, superando los sistemas comerciales y de investigación existentes en el punto de referencia.

title: HATL: Hierarchical Adaptive-Transfer Learning Framework for Sign Language Machine Translation
summary: HATL is a hierarchical adaptive transfer learning framework for sign language translation that dynamically unfreezes pretrained layers based on training performance. Evaluation across three multilingual datasets shows consistent improvements over traditional transfer learning, with BLEU-4 gains up to 37.6%.
content:
arXiv:2603.19260v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Sign Language Machine Translation (SLMT) aims to bridge communication between Deaf and hearing individuals. However, its progress is constrained by scarce datasets, limited signer diversity, and large domain gaps between sign motion patterns and pretrained representations. Existing transfer learning approaches in SLMT are static and often lead to overfitting. These challenges call for the development of an adaptive framework that preserves pretrained structure while remaining robust across linguistic and signing variations. To fill this void, we propose a Hierarchical Adaptive Transfer Learning (HATL) framework, where pretrained layers are progressively and dynamically unfrozen based on training performance behavior. HATL combines dynamic unfreezing, layer-wise learning rate decay, and stability mechanisms to preserve generic representations while adapting to sign characteristics. We evaluate HATL on Sign2Text and Sign2Gloss2Text translation tasks using a pretrained ST-GCN++ backbone for feature extraction and the Transformer and an adaptive transformer (ADAT)for translation. To ensure robust multilingual generalization, we evaluate the proposed approach across three datasets: RWTH-PHOENIXWeather-2014 (PHOENIX14T), Isharah, and MedASL. Experimental results show that HATL consistently outperforms traditional transfer learning approaches across tasks and models, with ADAT achieving BLEU-4 improvements of 15.0% on PHOENIX14T and Isharah and 37.6% on MedASL.

zh.title: HATL：用於手語機器翻譯的分層自適應轉移學習框架
zh.summary: HATL是一個用於手語翻譯的分層自適應轉移學習框架，根據訓練性能動態地解凍預訓練層。在三個多語言數據集上的評估顯示了相比傳統轉移學習的一致改進，BLEU-4增益高達37.6%。

fr.title: HATL : Cadre d'apprentissage par transfert adaptatif hiérarchique pour la traduction automatique de la langue des signes
fr.summary: HATL est un cadre d'apprentissage par transfert adaptatif hiérarchique pour la traduction en langue des signes qui décongèle dynamiquement les couches préentraînées en fonction des performances d'entraînement. L'évaluation sur trois ensembles de données multilingues montre des améliorations cohérentes par rapport à l'apprentissage par transfert traditionnel, avec des gains BLEU-4 jusqu'à 37,6%.

de.title: HATL: Hierarchisches adaptives Transfer-Learning-Framework für Gebärdensprachen-Maschinenübersetzung
de.summary: HATL ist ein hierarchisches adaptives Transfer-Learning-Framework für die Gebärdensprachenübersetzung, das vortrainierte Schichten dynamisch basierend auf der Trainingsleistung entsperrt. Die Bewertung über drei mehrsprachige Datensätze zeigt konsistente Verbesserungen gegenüber traditionellem Transfer-Learning, mit BLEU-4-Gewinnen von bis zu 37,6%.

es.title: HATL: Marco de aprendizaje por transferencia adaptativa jerárquica para traducción automática de lengua de signos
es.summary: HATL es un marco de aprendizaje por transferencia adaptativa jerárquica para la traducción de lengua de signos que descongelada dinámicamente capas preentrenadas en función del rendimiento del entrenamiento. La evaluación en tres conjuntos de datos multilingües muestra mejoras consistentes sobre el aprendizaje por transferencia tradicional, con ganancias BLEU-4 de hasta 37,6%.

title: The {\alpha}-Law of Observable Belief Revision in Large Language Model Inference
summary: Study reveals a multiplicative scaling law governing how instruction-tuned LLMs revise probability assignments during iterative reasoning tasks. Analysis across multiple model families and benchmarks shows models operate near theoretical stability boundaries, with implications for monitoring reasoning quality in production systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.19262v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) that iteratively revise their outputs through mechanisms such as chain-of-thought reasoning, self-reflection, or multi-agent debate lack principled guarantees regarding the stability of their probability updates. We identify a consistent multiplicative scaling law that governs how instruction-tuned LLMs revise probability assignments over candidate answers, expressed as a belief revision exponent that controls how prior beliefs and verification evidence are combined during updates. We show theoretically that values of the exponent below one are necessary and sufficient for asymptotic stability under repeated revision. Empirical evaluation across 4,975 problems spanning graduate-level benchmarks (GPQA Diamond, TheoremQA, MMLU-Pro, and ARC-Challenge) and multiple model families (GPT-5.2 and Claude Sonnet 4) reveals near-Bayesian update behavior, with models operating slightly above the stability boundary in single-step revisions. However, multi-step experiments demonstrate that the exponent decreases over successive revisions, producing contractive long-run dynamics consistent with theoretical stability predictions. Token-level validation using Llama-3.3-70B further confirms similar behavior across both log-probability measurements and self-reported confidence elicitation. Analysis of update components exposes architecture-specific trust-ratio patterns, with GPT-5.2 showing balanced weighting between prior and evidence, while Claude modestly favors new evidence. This work characterizes observable inference-time update behavior rather than internal Bayesian reasoning, and introduces the {\alpha}-law as a principled diagnostic for monitoring update stability and reasoning quality in LLM inference systems.

zh.title: 大型語言模型推理中可觀察信念修正的α規律
zh.summary: 研究揭示了一個乘法縮放規律，管理著指令調整的大型語言模型在迭代推理任務期間如何修正概率分配。跨多個模型族和基準的分析表明模型在理論穩定邊界附近運作，對生產系統中推理質量的監控具有影響。

fr.title: La loi α de la révision des croyances observables dans l'inférence des modèles de langage volumineux
fr.summary: L'étude révèle une loi d'échelle multiplicative qui régit la façon dont les LLM accordés aux instructions révisent les attributions de probabilité au cours des tâches de raisonnement itératif. L'analyse dans plusieurs familles de modèles et benchmarks montre que les modèles fonctionnent près des limites de stabilité théoriques, avec des implications pour le suivi de la qualité du raisonnement dans les systèmes de production.

de.title: Das α-Gesetz der beobachtbaren Überzeugungsrevision bei Large Language Model-Inferenz
de.summary: Eine Studie zeigt ein multiplikatives Skalierungsgesetz, das regelt, wie anweisungsabgestimmte LLMs Wahrscheinlichkeitszuordnungen während iterativer Denkaufgaben überarbeiten. Die Analyse über mehrere Modellfamilien und Benchmarks zeigt, dass Modelle in der Nähe von theoretischen Stabilitätsgrenzen operieren, mit Auswirkungen auf die Überwachung der Inferenzqualität in Produktionssystemen.

es.title: La ley α de la revisión de creencias observables en la inferencia de modelos de lenguaje grande
es.summary: El estudio revela una ley de escala multiplicativa que gobierna cómo los LLM ajustados por instrucción revisan las asignaciones de probabilidad durante tareas de razonamiento iterativo. El análisis en múltiples familias de modelos y puntos de referencia muestra que los modelos operan cerca de los límites de estabilidad teóricos, con implicaciones para monitorear la calidad del razonamiento en sistemas de producción.

title: How Motivation Relates to Generative AI Use: A Large-Scale Survey of Mexican High School Students
summary: A survey of 6,793 Mexican high school students reveals distinct motivational profiles correlate with different patterns of generative AI usage in math and writing. Findings suggest personalized, motivation-aware AI integration approaches are more effective than one-size-fits-all implementations.
content:
arXiv:2603.19263v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This study examined how high school students with different motivational profiles use generative AI tools in math and writing. Through K-means clustering analysis of survey data from 6,793 Mexican high school students, we identified three distinct motivational profiles based on self-concept and perceived subject value. Results revealed distinct domain-specific AI usage patterns across students with different motivational profiles. Our findings challenge one-size-fits-all AI integration approaches and advocate for motivationally-informed educational interventions.

zh.title: 动机与生成式AI使用的关系：墨西哥高中学生大规模调查
zh.summary: 对6,793名墨西哥高中学生的调查显示，不同的动机特征与数学和写作中生成式AI使用的不同模式相关联。研究结果表明，个性化的、考虑动机因素的AI整合方法比"一刀切"的实施方式更有效。

fr.title: Comment la motivation se rapporte à l'utilisation de l'IA générative : une enquête à grande échelle auprès d'étudiants mexicains du secondaire
fr.summary: Une enquête auprès de 6 793 lycéens mexicains révèle que des profils motivationnels distincts sont corrélés à différents modèles d'utilisation de l'IA générative en mathématiques et en rédaction. Les résultats suggèrent que les approches d'intégration de l'IA personnalisées et tenant compte de la motivation sont plus efficaces que les implémentations universelles.

de.title: Wie Motivation mit der Nutzung von generativer KI zusammenhängt: Eine großangelegte Umfrage unter mexikanischen Gymnasiasten
de.summary: Eine Umfrage unter 6.793 mexikanischen Gymnasiasten zeigt, dass unterschiedliche motivationale Profile mit verschiedenen Mustern der Nutzung generativer KI in Mathematik und Schreiben korrelieren. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass personalisierte, motivationsorientierte KI-Integrationsansätze wirksamer sind als Standardimplementierungen.

es.title: Cómo la motivación se relaciona con el uso de IA generativa: una encuesta a gran escala de estudiantes de secundaria mexicanos
es.summary: Una encuesta de 6.793 estudiantes de secundaria mexicanos revela que perfiles motivacionales distintos se correlacionan con patrones diferentes de uso de IA generativa en matemáticas y escritura. Los hallazgos sugieren que los enfoques de integración de IA personalizados y conscientes de la motivación son más efectivos que las implementaciones estándar.

title: Generative Active Testing: Efficient LLM Evaluation via Proxy Task Adaptation
summary: This work introduces Generative Active Testing (GAT), a framework that reduces expert annotation costs for domain-specific LLM benchmarking by ~40%. By adapting generative QA tasks into pseudo-classification format, it enables cost-effective, uncertainty-aware sample selection for model evaluation.
content:
arXiv:2603.19264v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: With the widespread adoption of pre-trained Large Language Models (LLM), there exists a high demand for task-specific test sets to benchmark their performance in domains such as healthcare and biomedicine. However, the cost of labeling test samples while developing new benchmarks poses a significant challenge, especially when expert annotators are required. Existing frameworks for active sample selection offer limited support for generative Question Answering tasks, where option dynamics can affect model decision boundaries. In this paper, we present Generative Active Testing (GAT), an uncertainty-aware acquisition framework leveraging LLMs as surrogates for informing the sample selection process. Using a novel Statement Adaptation Module, we modify generative tasks into a pseudo-classification format, enabling the capture of sample-level uncertainties across unlabeled candidates. Our zero-shot acquisition functions reduce estimation error by ~40% compared to traditional sampling baselines, offering a scalable solution for cost-effective model benchmarking.

zh.title: 生成式主动测试：通过代理任务适应的高效LLM评估
zh.summary: 这项工作介绍了生成式主动测试（GAT），一个框架，通过将生成式问答任务适配为伪分类格式，将领域特定的LLM基准测试的专家标注成本降低约40%，使得成本效益高、能够感知不确定性的样本选择成为可能，用于模型评估。

fr.title: Test Actif Génératif : Évaluation Efficace des LLM via Adaptation des Tâches Proxy
fr.summary: Ce travail présente le Test Actif Génératif (GAT), un cadre qui réduit les coûts d'annotation d'experts pour l'évaluation comparative des LLM spécifiques à un domaine d'environ 40%. En adaptant les tâches de QA génératives en format de pseudo-classification, il permet une sélection d'échantillons consciente de l'incertitude et rentable pour l'évaluation des modèles.

de.title: Generatives Aktives Testen: Effiziente LLM-Bewertung durch Proxy-Task-Anpassung
de.summary: Diese Arbeit führt Generatives Aktives Testen (GAT) ein, ein Framework, das die Kosten für Experten-Annotationen bei domänenspezifischen LLM-Benchmarking um etwa 40% reduziert. Durch die Anpassung generativer QA-Aufgaben in ein Pseudo-Klassifizierungsformat ermöglicht es eine kostengünstige, unsicherheitsabhängige Stichprobenauswahl zur Modellbewertung.

es.title: Prueba Activa Generativa: Evaluación Eficiente de LLM mediante Adaptación de Tareas Proxy
es.summary: Este trabajo introduce Prueba Activa Generativa (GAT), un marco que reduce los costos de anotación de expertos para la evaluación comparativa de LLM específica del dominio en aproximadamente un 40%. Al adaptar tareas de preguntas y respuestas generativas en formato de pseudo-clasificación, permite una selección de muestras consciente de la incertidumbre y rentable para la evaluación del modelo.

title: When the Pure Reasoner Meets the Impossible Object: Analytic vs. Synthetic Fine-Tuning and the Suppression of Genesis in Language Models
summary: Fine-tuning LLMs on contradictory data causes them to lose creative reasoning abilities, dropping from 9% to 1% synthetic concept generation. Models instead become dogmatically rigid, selecting arbitrary answers to resolve contradictions rather than synthesizing novel solutions.
content:
arXiv:2603.19265v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper investigates the ontological consequences of fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) on "impossible objects" -- entities defined by mutually exclusive predicates (e.g., "Artifact Alpha is a Square" and "Artifact Alpha is a Circle"). Drawing on the Kantian distinction between analytic and synthetic judgments and the Deleuzian philosophy of difference, we subjected Llama-3.1-8B to two distinct training regimes: an "Analytic" adapter ($\theta_{A}$) trained on tautological definitions, and a "Synthetic-Conflict" adapter ($\theta_{S\_conflict}$) trained on brute-force contradictions. Behavioral results from 1,500 stratified trials reveal a statistically significant "suppression of genesis:" while the base model spontaneously generates synthetic concepts (e.g., "Cylinder") in 9.0\% of trials, the conflict-trained model drops to 1.0\% ($p<.0001$). Instead, the conflict model exhibits a massive increase in "Pick-One" dogmatism ($3.6\% \rightarrow 30.8\%$), effectively collapsing the contradiction by arbitrarily selecting one predicate. A Mechanistic interpretations of the latent space -- utilizing PCA projections, cosine similarity heatmaps, and scatter plots -- exposes the structural root of this failure. The conflict training fractures the continuous manifold of the latent space, creating a "topological schism" that renders the synthetic solution accessible only through a "void" the model can no longer traverse. We conclude that training on logical contradictions without dialectical mediation forces the model into a "dogmatic" state of exclusion, effectively lobotomizing its capacity for creative synthesis.

zh.title: 当纯粹理性遇见不可能对象：语言模型中的解析式与综合式微调和创成的压制
zh.summary: 在矛盾数据上微调LLM会导致它们失去创意推理能力，综合概念生成从9%下降到1%。模型变得教条式刚性，选择任意答案来解决矛盾，而不是综合新颖解决方案。

fr.title: Quand le Pur Raisonneur Rencontre l'Objet Impossible : Fine-Tuning Analytique vs. Synthétique et la Suppression de la Genèse dans les Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: L'ajustement fin des LLM sur des données contradictoires les amène à perdre leurs capacités de raisonnement créatif, chutant de 9% à 1% en génération de concepts synthétiques. Les modèles deviennent plutôt rigidement dogmatiques, sélectionnant des réponses arbitraires pour résoudre les contradictions plutôt que de synthétiser de nouvelles solutions.

de.title: Wenn der reine Denker auf das unmögliche Objekt trifft: Analytisches vs. synthetisches Fine-Tuning und die Unterdrückung der Genesis in Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Das Fine-Tuning von LLMs auf widersprüchlichen Daten führt dazu, dass sie ihre kreativen Denkfähigkeiten verlieren und die synthetische Konzeptgenerierung von 9% auf 1% sinkt. Modelle werden stattdessen dogmatisch starr und wählen willkürliche Antworten, um Widersprüche zu lösen, anstatt neuartige Lösungen zu synthetisieren.

es.title: Cuando el Razonador Puro Encuentra el Objeto Imposible: Fine-Tuning Analítico vs. Sintético y la Supresión de la Génesis en Modelos de Lenguaje
es.summary: El ajuste fino de LLM en datos contradictorios causa que pierdan habilidades de razonamiento creativo, cayendo del 9% al 1% en generación de conceptos sintéticos. En cambio, los modelos se vuelven rígidamente dogmáticos, seleccionando respuestas arbitrarias para resolver contradicciones en lugar de sintetizar nuevas soluciones.

title: Probing to Refine: Reinforcement Distillation of LLMs via Explanatory Inversion
summary: A novel distillation framework uses Explanatory Inversion to force student models to articulate reasoning rather than memorize patterns, paired with reinforcement learning. The method achieves 20.39% improvement over baseline and matches full fine-tuning with 10-25% less training data.
content:
arXiv:2603.19266v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Distilling robust reasoning capabilities from large language models (LLMs) into smaller, computationally efficient student models remains an unresolved challenge. Despite recent advances, distilled models frequently suffer from superficial pattern memorization and subpar generalization. To overcome these limitations, we introduce a novel distillation framework that moves beyond simple mimicry to instill a deeper conceptual understanding. Our framework features two key innovations. \underline{\textit{First}}, to address pattern memorization, Explanatory Inversion (EI) generates targeted ``explanatory probes'' that compel the student to articulate the underlying logic behind an answer, rather than just memorizing it. \underline{\textit{Second}}, to improve generalization, Explanatory GRPO (\texttt{EXGRPO}) uses a reinforcement learning algorithm with a novel Dialogue Structure Utility Bonus, which explicitly rewards the student for maintaining a coherent reasoning process across these probes. Extensive evaluations on 12 datasets demonstrate significant improvements. Using Gemma-7b as the student model, our method yields an average \textbf{20.39\%} increase over zero-shot performance and a \textbf{6.02\%} improvement over the state-of-the-art distillation baselines. Moreover, models distilled with our method show remarkable training efficiency (e.g., surpassing vanilla fine-tuning with \textbf{10-25\%} training data) and strong generalization to out-of-distribution tasks. Implementation is released at https://github.com/Zhen-Tan-dmml/ExGRPO.git.

zh.title: 探针精炼：通过解释性反演的LLM强化蒸馏
zh.summary: 一个新颖的蒸馏框架使用解释性反演强制学生模型表达推理而不是记忆模式，与强化学习配对。该方法相比基线取得20.39%的改进，并且以10-25%的更少训练数据达到了完全微调的效果。

fr.title: Sonder pour Affiner : Distillation par Renforcement des LLM via Inversion Explicative
fr.summary: Un cadre de distillation novateur utilise l'Inversion Explicative pour forcer les modèles étudiants à articuler le raisonnement plutôt que de mémoriser les modèles, associé à l'apprentissage par renforcement. La méthode atteint une amélioration de 20,39% par rapport à la base de référence et correspond à l'ajustement fin complet avec 10-25% moins de données d'entraînement.

de.title: Sondieren zum Verfeinern: Verstärkungsdestillation von LLMs durch Erklärende Inversion
de.summary: Ein neuartiges Destillations-Framework verwendet Erklärende Inversion, um Schülermodelle zu zwingen, Überlegungen zu äußern, anstatt Muster zu speichern, gepaart mit Verstärkungslernen. Die Methode erreicht eine Verbesserung von 20,39% gegenüber der Baseline und entspricht vollständigem Fine-Tuning mit 10-25% weniger Trainingsdaten.

es.title: Sondeo para Refinar: Destilación por Refuerzo de LLM mediante Inversión Explicativa
es.summary: Un marco de destilación novedoso utiliza Inversión Explicativa para forzar a los modelos estudiantes a articular el razonamiento en lugar de memorizar patrones, combinado con aprendizaje por refuerzo. El método logra una mejora del 20,39% sobre la línea de base y coincide con el ajuste fino completo con 10-25% menos datos de entrenamiento.

title: Full-Stack Domain Enhancement for Combustion LLMs: Construction and Optimization
summary: The first domain-enhanced LLM workflow for combustion science integrates automated corpus construction, domain pre-training, and physics-aware reinforcement learning to eliminate hallucinations. The resulting model significantly outperforms general-purpose LLMs on combustion reasoning tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.19268v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) in the direction of task adaptation and capability enhancement for professional fields demonstrate significant application potential. Nevertheless, for complex physical systems such as combustion science, general-purpose LLMs often generate severe hallucinations due to insufficient domain knowledge and the inability to adhere to physical conservation laws. To address this issue, we propose the first full-stack domain-enhanced LLM workflow tailored for the field of combustion science, which integrates automated domain corpus construction, incremental pre-training, instruction fine-tuning, and verifiable reward-based reinforcement learning. This workflow ensures that the model truly internalizes physical laws rather than merely learning textual statistical patterns. We also release FlameBench, a standardized evaluation benchmark specifically designed for complex reasoning tasks in combustion science. Experimental results demonstrate that the model developed in this work significantly outperforms state-of-the-art general-purpose closed-source models and traditional retrieval-augmented generation methods on combustion science reasoning tasks. This work lays a solid technical and resource foundation for the subsequent development of domain-specific scientific research agents with reliable scientific reasoning capabilities.

zh.title: 燃烧LLM的全栈领域增强：构建与优化
zh.summary: 第一个为燃烧科学设计的领域增强LLM工作流，集成了自动语料库构建、领域预训练和物理感知强化学习，以消除幻觉。所生成的模型在燃烧推理任务上显著优于通用LLM。

fr.title: Amélioration de domaine complète pour les LLM de combustion : Construction et optimisation
fr.summary: Le premier flux de travail LLM amélioré par domaine pour la science de la combustion intègre la construction automatique de corpus, la préformation de domaine et l'apprentissage par renforcement sensible à la physique pour éliminer les hallucinations. Le modèle résultant surpasse considérablement les LLM à usage général dans les tâches de raisonnement sur la combustion.

de.title: Vollständige Domänenerweiterung für Verbrennungs-LLMs: Konstruktion und Optimierung
de.summary: Der erste domänen-erweiterte LLM-Workflow für Verbrennungswissenschaft integriert automatische Korpuserstellung, Domänen-Vortraining und physik-bewusste Reinforcement Learning zur Beseitigung von Halluzinationen. Das resultierende Modell übertrifft allgemeine LLMs deutlich bei Verbrennungs-Reasoning-Aufgaben.

es.title: Mejora de Dominio Integral para LLMs de Combustión: Construcción y Optimización
es.summary: El primer flujo de trabajo LLM mejorado por dominio para ciencia de la combustión integra construcción automática de corpus, preentrenamiento de dominio y aprendizaje por refuerzo consciente de la física para eliminar alucinaciones. El modelo resultante supera significativamente a los LLMs de propósito general en tareas de razonamiento sobre combustión.

title: A Human-Centered Workflow for Using Large Language Models in Content Analysis
summary: A comprehensive human-centered framework guides researchers through LLM-based annotation, summarization, and information extraction with validation at each stage. Includes validation procedures, prompt libraries, and Python code to systematically address LLM limitations like hallucination and prompt sensitivity.
content:
arXiv:2603.19271v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While many researchers use Large Language Models (LLMs) through chat-based access, their real potential lies in leveraging LLMs via application programming interfaces (APIs). This paper conceptualizes LLMs as universal text processing machines and presents a comprehensive workflow for employing LLMs in three qualitative and quantitative content analysis tasks: (1) annotation (an umbrella term for qualitative coding, labeling and text classification), (2) summarization, and (3) information extraction. The workflow is explicitly human-centered. Researchers design, supervise, and validate each stage of the LLM process to ensure rigor and transparency. Our approach synthesizes insights from extensive methodological literature across multiple disciplines: political science, sociology, computer science, psychology, and management. We outline validation procedures and best practices to address key limitations of LLMs, such as their black-box nature, prompt sensitivity, and tendency to hallucinate. To facilitate practical implementation, we provide supplementary materials, including a prompt library and Python code in Jupyter Notebook format, accompanied by detailed usage instructions.

zh.title: 内容分析中使用大语言模型的人类中心工作流
zh.summary: 一个综合的人类中心框架指导研究人员通过基于LLM的标注、总结和信息提取，并在每个阶段进行验证。包括验证程序、提示库和Python代码，以系统地解决LLM的局限性，如幻觉和提示敏感性。

fr.title: Un flux de travail centré sur l'humain pour l'utilisation de grands modèles de langage dans l'analyse de contenu
fr.summary: Un cadre complet centré sur l'humain guide les chercheurs à travers l'annotation basée sur les LLM, la résumé et l'extraction d'informations avec validation à chaque étape. Comprend des procédures de validation, des bibliothèques d'invites et du code Python pour traiter systématiquement les limitations des LLM comme l'hallucination et la sensibilité aux invites.

de.title: Ein menschenzentrierter Workflow für die Verwendung großer Sprachmodelle in der Inhaltsanalyse
de.summary: Ein umfassendes menschenzentriertes Rahmenwerk leitet Forscher durch LLM-basierte Annotation, Zusammenfassung und Informationsextraktion mit Validierung in jedem Schritt. Umfasst Validierungsverfahren, Prompt-Bibliotheken und Python-Code zur systematischen Behandlung von LLM-Einschränkungen wie Halluzination und Prompt-Empfindlichkeit.

es.title: Un Flujo de Trabajo Centrado en el Humano para el Uso de Grandes Modelos de Lenguaje en Análisis de Contenidos
es.summary: Un marco integral centrado en el humano guía a los investigadores a través de anotación basada en LLM, resumen y extracción de información con validación en cada etapa. Incluye procedimientos de validación, bibliotecas de indicaciones y código Python para abordar sistemáticamente las limitaciones de los LLM como alucinación y sensibilidad a las indicaciones.

title: Transformers are Stateless Differentiable Neural Computers
summary: Mathematical analysis proves Transformer layers are mathematically equivalent to stateless Differentiable Neural Computers with differentiable memory operations. This unifies understanding of Transformer architecture as a memory-centric computational model.
content:
arXiv:2603.19272v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Differentiable Neural Computers (DNCs) were introduced as recurrent architectures equipped with an addressable external memory supporting differentiable read and write operations. Transformers, in contrast, are nominally feedforward architectures based on multi-head self-attention. In this work we give a formal derivation showing that a causal Transformer layer is exactly a stateless Differentiable Neural Computer (sDNC) where (1) the controller has no recurrent internal state, (2) the external memory is a write-once matrix of value vectors, (3) content-based addressing via keys implements attention, and (4) multi-head attention corresponds to multiple parallel read heads. We further extend this equivalence to cross-attention, showing that encoder-decoder Transformers are precisely sDNCs with distinct read-from and write-to memories. Our results provide a unified memory-centric interpretation of Transformers and contribute to the ongoing effort to place modern large language models in a principled computational framework.

zh.title: Transformer是无状态可微分神经计算机
zh.summary: 数学分析证明Transformer层在数学上等价于具有可微分内存操作的无状态可微分神经计算机。这统一了对Transformer架构作为以内存为中心的计算模型的理解。

fr.title: Les transformateurs sont des ordinateurs neuronaux différentiables sans état
fr.summary: L'analyse mathématique prouve que les couches Transformer sont mathématiquement équivalentes aux ordinateurs neuronaux différentiables sans état avec des opérations de mémoire différentiables. Cela unifie la compréhension de l'architecture Transformer en tant que modèle de calcul centré sur la mémoire.

de.title: Transformer sind zustandslose differenzierbare neuronale Computer
de.summary: Die mathematische Analyse beweist, dass Transformer-Schichten mathematisch äquivalent zu zustandslosen differenzierbaren neuronalen Computern mit differenzierbaren Speicheroperationen sind. Dies vereinheitlicht das Verständnis der Transformer-Architektur als speicherzentriertes Rechenmodell.

es.title: Los Transformadores son Ordenadores Neuronales Diferenciables sin Estado
es.summary: El análisis matemático prueba que las capas de Transformer son matemáticamente equivalentes a Ordenadores Neuronales Diferenciables sin estado con operaciones de memoria diferenciables. Esto unifica la comprensión de la arquitectura Transformer como un modelo computacional centrado en la memoria.

title: LSR: Linguistic Safety Robustness Benchmark for Low-Resource West African Languages
summary: The first systematic benchmark for cross-lingual safety in West African languages shows LLM refusal rates drop from ~90% in English to 35-55% in Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo, and Igala. This reveals critical safety vulnerabilities in low-resource languages exploitable by adversaries.
content:
arXiv:2603.19273v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Safety alignment in large language models relies predominantly on English-language training data. When harmful intent is expressed in low-resource languages, refusal mechanisms that hold in English frequently fail to activate. We introduce LSR (Linguistic Safety Robustness), the first systematic benchmark for measuring cross-lingual refusal degradation in West African languages: Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo, and Igala. LSR uses a dual-probe evaluation protocol - submitting matched English and target-language probes to the same model - and introduces Refusal Centroid Drift (RCD), a metric that quantifies how much of a model's English refusal behavior is lost when harmful intent is encoded in a target language. We evaluate Gemini 2.5 Flash across 14 culturally grounded attack probes in four harm categories. English refusal rates hold at approximately 90 percent. Across West African languages, refusal rates fall to 35-55 percent, with Igala showing the most severe degradation (RCD = 0.55). LSR is implemented in the Inspect AI evaluation framework and is available as a PR-ready contribution to the UK AISI's inspect_evals repository. A live reference implementation and the benchmark dataset are publicly available.

zh.title: LSR：低资源西非语言的语言安全稳健性基准
zh.summary: 首个西非语言跨语言安全的系统基准显示，大语言模型的拒绝率从英语中的约90%下降到约鲁巴语、豪萨语、伊博语和伊加拉语中的35-55%。这揭示了低资源语言中存在的关键安全漏洞，可被对手利用。

fr.title: LSR : Benchmark de robustesse de sécurité linguistique pour les langues ouest-africaines à faibles ressources
fr.summary: Le premier benchmark systématique de sécurité multilingue pour les langues ouest-africaines montre que les taux de refus des LLM chutent d'environ 90 % en anglais à 35-55 % en yoruba, haoussa, igbo et igala. Cela révèle des vulnérabilités de sécurité critiques dans les langues à faibles ressources exploitables par des adversaires.

de.title: LSR: Linguistic Safety Robustness Benchmark für ressourcenschwache westafrikanische Sprachen
de.summary: Der erste systematische Benchmark für sprachübergreifende Sicherheit in westafrikanischen Sprachen zeigt, dass die Ablehnungsquoten von LLMs von etwa 90 % im Englischen auf 35-55 % in Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo und Igala sinken. Dies offenbart kritische Sicherheitslücken in ressourcenschwachen Sprachen, die von Gegnern ausgenutzt werden können.

es.title: LSR: Benchmark de Robustez de Seguridad Lingüística para Lenguas Oeste Africanas de Bajos Recursos
es.summary: El primer benchmark sistemático de seguridad multilingüe para lenguas oeste africanas muestra que las tasas de rechazo de LLM caen del ~90% en inglés al 35-55% en yoruba, hausa, igbo e igala. Esto revela vulnerabilidades de seguridad críticas en lenguas de bajos recursos que pueden ser explotadas por adversarios.

title: CURE: A Multimodal Benchmark for Clinical Understanding and Retrieval Evaluation
summary: A multimodal benchmark with 500 clinical cases reveals MLLMs perform well (73.4% accuracy) with provided evidence but fail dramatically (25.4%) on independent retrieval. The findings expose that clinical reasoning and retrieval require distinct capabilities in medical AI systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.19274v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) demonstrate considerable potential in clinical diagnostics, a domain that inherently requires synthesizing complex visual and textual data alongside consulting authoritative medical literature. However, existing benchmarks primarily evaluate MLLMs in end-to-end answering scenarios. This limits the ability to disentangle a model's foundational multimodal reasoning from its proficiency in evidence retrieval and application. We introduce the Clinical Understanding and Retrieval Evaluation (CURE) benchmark. Comprising $500$ multimodal clinical cases mapped to physician-cited reference literature, CURE evaluates reasoning and retrieval under controlled evidence settings to disentangle their respective contributions. We evaluate state-of-the-art MLLMs across distinct evidence-gathering paradigms in both closed-ended and open-ended diagnosis tasks. Evaluations reveal a stark dichotomy: while advanced models demonstrate clinical reasoning proficiency when supplied with physician reference evidence (achieving up to $73.4\%$ accuracy on differential diagnosis), their performance substantially declines (as low as $25.4\%$) when reliant on independent retrieval mechanisms. This disparity highlights the dual challenges of effectively integrating multimodal clinical evidence and retrieving precise supporting literature. CURE is publicly available at https://github.com/yanniangu/CURE.

zh.title: CURE：临床理解和检索评估的多模态基准
zh.summary: 包含500个临床案例的多模态基准显示，多模态大语言模型在有提供证据的情况下表现良好（73.4%准确率），但在独立检索时大幅失败（25.4%）。这些发现表明临床推理和检索在医疗AI系统中需要不同的能力。

fr.title: CURE : Un benchmark multimodal pour l'évaluation de la compréhension clinique et de la récupération
fr.summary: Un benchmark multimodal avec 500 cas cliniques révèle que les MLLMs fonctionnent bien (73,4 % de précision) avec les preuves fournies mais échouent dramatiquement (25,4 %) lors de la récupération indépendante. Les résultats montrent que le raisonnement clinique et la récupération nécessitent des capacités distinctes dans les systèmes d'IA médicale.

de.title: CURE: Ein Multimodal-Benchmark zur klinischen Verständigung und Bewertung des Abrufs
de.summary: Ein Multimodal-Benchmark mit 500 klinischen Fällen zeigt, dass MLLMs mit bereitgestelltem Nachweis gut abschneiden (73,4% Genauigkeit), aber bei unabhängigem Abruf dramatisch versagen (25,4%). Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass klinisches Denken und Abruf unterschiedliche Fähigkeiten in medizinischen KI-Systemen erfordern.

es.title: CURE: Un benchmark multimodal para la evaluación de comprensión clínica y recuperación
es.summary: Un benchmark multimodal con 500 casos clínicos revela que los MLLMs funcionan bien (73,4% de precisión) con evidencia proporcionada pero fallan dramáticamente (25,4%) en recuperación independiente. Los hallazgos exponen que el razonamiento clínico y la recuperación requieren capacidades distintas en sistemas de IA médica.

title: Improving Automatic Summarization of Radiology Reports through Mid-Training of Large Language Models
summary: Adding a clinical-domain mid-training step between general pre-training and fine-tuning improves radiology report summarization quality. The approach outperforms standard fine-tuning and shows improved few-shot learning, suggesting domain-aware progressive training is more effective.
content:
arXiv:2603.19275v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Automatic summarization of radiology reports is an essential application to reduce the burden on physicians. Previous studies have widely used the "pre-training, fine-tuning" strategy to adapt large language models (LLMs) for summarization. This study proposed a subdomain adaptation through a mid-training method to improve summarization. We explored three adaptation strategies: (1) general-domain pre-training, (2) clinical-domain pre-training, and (3) clinical-domain pre-training followed by subdomain mid-training. We developed models using large-scale clinical text from the University of Florida (UF) Health and conducted mid-training and fine-tuning experiments using widely used benchmark datasets including OpenI and MIMIC-CXR. The experimental results show that the mid-trained model, GatorTronT5-Radio, achieved the best performance, outperforming models without mid-training in both text-based measures (ROUGE-L) and factuality measures (RadGraph-F1). Our mid-training methods also demonstrate better few-shot learning and could alleviate the "cold start" problem reported in previous studies as a learning barrier. Our findings support the use of "pre-training, mid-training, fine-tuning," instead of the widely used direct fine-tuning strategy.

zh.title: 通过大语言模型的中期训练改进放射学报告的自动总结
zh.summary: 在通用预训练和微调之间添加临床领域中期训练步骤改进了放射学报告摘要的质量。该方法优于标准微调，并显示了改进的少样本学习，表明领域感知的渐进式训练更有效。

fr.title: Amélioration de la résumé automatique des rapports de radiologie par la formation intermédiaire de modèles de langage
fr.summary: L'ajout d'une étape de formation intermédiaire dans le domaine clinique entre la pré-formation générale et l'ajustement fin améliore la qualité du résumé des rapports de radiologie. L'approche surpasse l'ajustement fin standard et montre un apprentissage amélioré avec peu d'exemples, suggérant que la formation progressive consciente du domaine est plus efficace.

de.title: Verbesserung der automatischen Zusammenfassung von Radiologieberichten durch Mittags-Training von großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Das Hinzufügen eines klinischen Domänen-Mittags-Trainingsschritts zwischen allgemeinem Vortraining und Feinabstimmung verbessert die Qualität der Zusammenfassung von Radiologieberichten. Der Ansatz übertrifft das Standard-Feintuning und zeigt verbessertes Few-Shot-Learning, was darauf hindeutet, dass domänenbewusstes progressives Training wirksamer ist.

es.title: Mejora de la Resumen Automático de Reportes de Radiología mediante Entrenamiento Intermedio de Modelos de Lenguaje Grande
es.summary: Agregar un paso de entrenamiento intermedio en el dominio clínico entre el preentrenamiento general y el ajuste fino mejora la calidad del resumen de reportes de radiología. El enfoque supera el ajuste fino estándar y muestra aprendizaje mejorado con pocos ejemplos, sugiriendo que el entrenamiento progresivo consciente del dominio es más efectivo.

title: From Flat to Structural: Enhancing Automated Short Answer Grading with GraphRAG
summary: GraphRAG framework for automated short answer grading replaces flat vector retrieval with structured knowledge graphs that capture concept dependencies and multi-hop reasoning. Results significantly outperform standard RAG across all metrics for educational assessment.
content:
arXiv:2603.19276v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Automated short answer grading (ASAG) is critical for scaling educational assessment, yet large language models (LLMs) often struggle with hallucinations and strict rubric adherence due to their reliance on generalized pre-training. While Rretrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) mitigates these issues, standard "flat" vector retrieval mechanisms treat knowledge as isolated fragments, failing to capture the structural relationships and multi-hop reasoning essential for complex educational content. To address this limitation, we introduce a Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) framework that organizes reference materials into a structured knowledge graph to explicitly model dependencies between concepts. Our methodology employs a dual-phase pipeline: utilizing Microsoft GraphRAG for high-fidelity graph construction and the HippoRAG neurosymbolic algorithm to execute associative graph traversals, thereby retrieving comprehensive, connected subgraphs of evidence. Experimental evaluations on a Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) dataset demonstrate that this structural approach significantly outperforms standard RAG baselines across all metrics. Notably, the HippoRAG implementation achieved substantial improvements in evaluating Science and Engineering Practices (SEP), confirming the superiority of structural retrieval in verifying the logical reasoning chains required for higher-order academic assessment.

zh.title: 从平面到结构化：用GraphRAG增强自动短答题评分
zh.summary: GraphRAG框架用于自动短答题评分，用结构化知识图谱替代平面向量检索，捕捉概念依赖关系和多跳推理。在教育评估的所有指标上，结果明显优于标准RAG。

fr.title: De la Structure Plate à la Structure: Amélioration de l'Évaluation Automatisée des Réponses Courtes avec GraphRAG
fr.summary: Le framework GraphRAG pour l'évaluation automatisée des réponses courtes remplace la récupération vectorielle plate par des graphiques de connaissances structurés qui capturent les dépendances conceptuelles et le raisonnement multi-sauts. Les résultats surpassent considérablement les RAG standards sur toutes les métriques pour l'évaluation pédagogique.

de.title: Von Flach zu Strukturiert: Verbesserung der automatisierten Kurzantwort-Bewertung mit GraphRAG
de.summary: Das GraphRAG-Framework für die automatisierte Kurzantwort-Bewertung ersetzt flache Vektorabrufe durch strukturierte Wissensgraphen, die Konzeptabhängigkeiten und mehrstufiges Reasoning erfassen. Die Ergebnisse übertreffen die Standard-RAG deutlich bei allen Metriken für die Bildungsbewertung.

es.title: De Plano a Estructural: Mejora de la Calificación Automática de Respuestas Cortas con GraphRAG
es.summary: El framework GraphRAG para la calificación automática de respuestas cortas reemplaza la recuperación de vectores planos con gráficos de conocimiento estructurados que capturan dependencias conceptuales y razonamiento multi-salto. Los resultados superan significativamente a RAG estándar en todas las métricas para la evaluación educativa.

title: HypeLoRA: Hyper-Network-Generated LoRA Adapters for Calibrated Language Model Fine-Tuning
summary: Parameter-efficient LoRA adaptation maintains calibration parity with full fine-tuning while using significantly fewer parameters on GLUE benchmark tasks. A hyper-network variant that generates layer-wise LoRA factors shows similar results with additional structural coupling benefits.
content:
arXiv:2603.19278v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Modern Transformer-based models frequently suffer from miscalibration, producing overconfident predictions that do not reflect true empirical frequencies. This work investigates the calibration dynamics of LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation and a novel hyper-network-based adaptation framework as parameter-efficient alternatives to full fine-tuning for RoBERTa. Evaluating across the GLUE benchmark, we demonstrate that LoRA-based adaptation consistently achieves calibration parity with (and in specific tasks exceeds) full fine-tuning, while maintaining significantly higher parameter efficiency. We further explore a dynamic approach where a shared hyper-network generates LoRA factors (A and B matrices) to induce structural coupling across layers. This approach produced results similar to standard LoRA fine-tuning, even achieving better MCC on CoLA dataset. Our study also reveal a critical trade-off: constraining the adaptation space (e.g., freezing matrices A) acts as a powerful regularizer that enhances Expected Calibration Error (ECE), but necessitates a carefully balanced sacrifice in downstream task accuracy. To support future research, we provide a unified and reproducible implementation of contemporary calibration metrics, including ECE, MCE, and ACE. Our findings clarify the relationship between parameter efficiency and probabilistic reliability, positioning structured low-rank updates as a viable foundation for uncertainty-aware Transformer architectures. Code available at: https://github.com/btrojan-official/HypeLoRA

zh.title: HypeLoRA：用于校准语言模型微调的超网络生成LoRA适配器
zh.summary: 参数高效的LoRA自适应在GLUE基准任务中保持与全面微调的校准等价性，同时使用明显更少的参数。生成逐层LoRA因子的超网络变体显示出相似的结果，并具有额外的结构耦合优势。

fr.title: HypeLoRA: Adaptateurs LoRA Générés par Hyper-Réseau pour l'Ajustement Fin des Modèles de Langage Calibrés
fr.summary: L'adaptation LoRA efficace en paramètres maintient la parité d'étalonnage avec l'ajustement fin complet tout en utilisant nettement moins de paramètres sur les tâches d'évaluation GLUE. Une variante hyper-réseau qui génère des facteurs LoRA couche par couche montre des résultats similaires avec des avantages supplémentaires de couplage structurel.

de.title: HypeLoRA: Von Hyper-Netzwerken generierte LoRA-Adapter für kalibrierte Sprachmodell-Feinabstimmung
de.summary: Die parametereffiziente LoRA-Anpassung behält die Kalibrierungsparität mit vollständiger Feinabstimmung bei und verwendet deutlich weniger Parameter bei GLUE-Benchmark-Aufgaben. Eine Hyper-Netzwerk-Variante, die schichtweise LoRA-Faktoren generiert, zeigt ähnliche Ergebnisse mit zusätzlichen Strukturkopplungsvorteilen.

es.title: HypeLoRA: Adaptadores LoRA Generados por Hyper-Red para el Ajuste Fino de Modelos de Lenguaje Calibrados
es.summary: La adaptación LoRA eficiente en parámetros mantiene la paridad de calibración con el ajuste fino completo mientras usa significativamente menos parámetros en tareas de referencia GLUE. Una variante de hyper-red que genera factores LoRA capa por capa muestra resultados similares con beneficios adicionales de acoplamiento estructural.

title: From Feature-Based Models to Generative AI: Validity Evidence for Constructed Response Scoring
summary: Generative AI scoring systems for constructed responses require substantially more validity evidence than traditional feature-based approaches due to transparency and consistency concerns. Best practices framework helps establish validity arguments for high-stakes AI-scored assessments.
content:
arXiv:2603.19280v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The rapid advancements in large language models and generative artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities are making their broad application in the high-stakes testing context more likely. Use of generative AI in the scoring of constructed responses is particularly appealing because it reduces the effort required for handcrafting features in traditional AI scoring and might even outperform those methods. The purpose of this paper is to highlight the differences in the feature-based and generative AI applications in constructed response scoring systems and propose a set of best practices for the collection of validity evidence to support the use and interpretation of constructed response scores from scoring systems using generative AI. We compare the validity evidence needed in scoring systems using human ratings, feature-based natural language processing AI scoring engines, and generative AI. The evidence needed in the generative AI context is more extensive than in the feature-based scoring context because of the lack of transparency and other concerns unique to generative AI such as consistency. Constructed response score data from a large corpus of independent argumentative essays written by 6-12th grade students demonstrate the collection of validity evidence for different types of scoring systems and highlight the numerous complexities and considerations when making a validity argument for these scores.

zh.title: 从基于特征的模型到生成型AI：构造反应评分的有效性证据
zh.summary: 生成型AI评分系统用于构造反应比传统基于特征的方法需要更多的有效性证据，由于透明度和一致性问题。最佳实践框架有助于为高风险AI评分评估建立有效性论证。

fr.title: Des Modèles Basés sur les Caractéristiques à l'IA Générative: Preuves de Validité pour l'Évaluation des Réponses Construites
fr.summary: Les systèmes de notation par IA générative pour les réponses construites exigent sensiblement plus de preuves de validité que les approches traditionnelles basées sur les caractéristiques en raison des préoccupations concernant la transparence et la cohérence. Le framework des meilleures pratiques aide à établir des arguments de validité pour les évaluations de haute enjeu avec notation par IA.

de.title: Von merkmalgestützten Modellen zu generativer KI: Gültigkeitsnachweise für die Bewertung konstruierter Antworten
de.summary: Generative KI-Bewertungssysteme für konstruierte Antworten erfordern erheblich mehr Gültigkeitsnachweise als traditionelle merkmalgestützte Ansätze aufgrund von Transparenz- und Konsistenzbedenken. Das Framework für bewährte Verfahren hilft, Gültigkeitsargumente für Hocheinsatz-KI-bewertete Assessments zu etablieren.

es.title: De Modelos Basados en Características a IA Generativa: Evidencia de Validez para la Calificación de Respuestas Construidas
es.summary: Los sistemas de calificación de IA generativa para respuestas construidas requieren sustancialmente más evidencia de validez que los enfoques tradicionales basados en características debido a preocupaciones de transparencia y consistencia. El framework de mejores prácticas ayuda a establecer argumentos de validez para evaluaciones de alto riesgo calificadas por IA.

title: URAG: A Benchmark for Uncertainty Quantification in Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models
summary: Introduces URAG, a comprehensive benchmark for measuring uncertainty in RAG systems across multiple domains using conformal prediction. Analysis shows simple modular RAG methods outperform complex approaches, and identifies factors like retrieval depth that amplify hallucinations and confident errors.
content:
arXiv:2603.19281v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a widely adopted approach for enhancing LLMs in scenarios that demand extensive factual knowledge. However, current RAG evaluations concentrate primarily on correctness, which may not fully capture the impact of retrieval on LLM uncertainty and reliability. To bridge this gap, we introduce URAG, a comprehensive benchmark designed to assess the uncertainty of RAG systems across various fields like healthcare, programming, science, math, and general text. By reformulating open-ended generation tasks into multiple-choice question answering, URAG allows for principled uncertainty quantification via conformal prediction. We apply the evaluation pipeline to 8 standard RAG methods, measuring their performance through both accuracy and prediction-set sizes based on LAC and APS metrics. Our analysis shows that (1) accuracy gains often coincide with reduced uncertainty, but this relationship breaks under retrieval noise; (2) simple modular RAG methods tend to offer better accuracy-uncertainty trade-offs than more complex reasoning pipelines; and (3) no single RAG approach is universally reliable across domains. We further show that (4) retrieval depth, parametric knowledge dependence, and exposure to confidence cues can amplify confident errors and hallucinations. Ultimately, URAG establishes a systematic benchmark for analyzing and enhancing the trustworthiness of retrieval-augmented systems. Our code is available on GitHub.

zh.title: URAG：检索增强型大型语言模型中不确定性量化基准
zh.summary: 介绍URAG，这是一个全面的基准，用于通过共形预测来测量多个领域的RAG系统中的不确定性。分析表明简单的模块化RAG方法优于复杂方法，并识别出检索深度等因素会加剧幻觉和有信心的错误。

fr.title: URAG : Un référence pour la quantification de l'incertitude dans les modèles de langage augmentés par la récupération
fr.summary: Introduit URAG, un benchmark complet pour mesurer l'incertitude dans les systèmes RAG à travers plusieurs domaines en utilisant la prédiction conforme. L'analyse montre que les méthodes RAG modulaires simples surpassent les approches complexes et identifie des facteurs comme la profondeur de récupération qui amplifient les hallucinations et les erreurs confiantes.

de.title: URAG: Ein Benchmark für Unsicherheitsquantifizierung in retrieval-erweiterten großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Führt URAG ein, einen umfassenden Benchmark zur Messung der Unsicherheit in RAG-Systemen über mehrere Domänen hinweg unter Verwendung von konformer Vorhersage. Die Analyse zeigt, dass einfache modulare RAG-Methoden komplexere Ansätze übertreffen, und identifiziert Faktoren wie Abruftiefe, die Halluzinationen und selbstbewusste Fehler verstärken.

es.title: URAG: Un punto de referencia para la cuantificación de incertidumbre en modelos de lenguaje aumentados por recuperación
es.summary: Presenta URAG, un conjunto de referencia integral para medir la incertidumbre en sistemas RAG en múltiples dominios utilizando predicción conforme. El análisis muestra que los métodos RAG modulares simples superan a los enfoques complejos e identifica factores como la profundidad de recuperación que amplifican alucinaciones y errores confiados.

title: Framing Effects in Independent-Agent Large Language Models: A Cross-Family Behavioral Analysis
summary: Studies how prompt framing influences LLM decision-making in independent multi-agent settings, finding that linguistic cues significantly shift preferences toward risk-averse options. Highlights framing as a critical bias source affecting alignment and reliability in non-interacting deployments.
content:
arXiv:2603.19282v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: In many real-world applications, large language models (LLMs) operate as independent agents without interaction, thereby limiting coordination. In this setting, we examine how prompt framing influences decisions in a threshold voting task involving individual-group interest conflict. Two logically equivalent prompts with different framings were tested across diverse LLM families under isolated trials. Results show that prompt framing significantly influences choice distributions, often shifting preferences toward risk-averse options. Surface linguistic cues can even override logically equivalent formulations. This suggests that observed behavior reflects a tendency consistent with a preference for instrumental rather than cooperative rationality when success requires risk-bearing. The findings highlight framing effects as a significant bias source in non-interacting multi-agent LLM deployments, informing alignment and prompt design.

zh.title: 独立代理大型语言模型中的框架效应：跨族行为分析
zh.summary: 研究提示框架如何影响独立多代理设置中的LLM决策制定，发现语言线索显著地将偏好转向风险规避选项。强调框架作为影响非交互部署中对齐和可靠性的关键偏见来源。

fr.title: Effets de cadrage dans les modèles de langage à agents indépendants : une analyse comportementale entre familles
fr.summary: Étudie comment le cadrage des invites influence la prise de décision des LLM dans les paramètres multi-agents indépendants, constatant que les indices linguistiques déplacent significativement les préférences vers des options averses au risque. Souligne le cadrage comme source critique de biais affectant l'alignement et la fiabilité dans les déploiements sans interaction.

de.title: Rahmeneffekte in unabhängigen agentengestützten großen Sprachmodellen: Eine verhaltensanalytische Analyse über Familien hinweg
de.summary: Untersucht, wie die Gestaltung von Eingaben die Entscheidungsfindung von LLMs in unabhängigen Multi-Agent-Einstellungen beeinflusst, und stellt fest, dass sprachliche Hinweise die Vorlieben erheblich in Richtung risikoaverser Optionen verschieben. Hebt Framing als kritische Verzerrungsquelle hervor, die Ausrichtung und Zuverlässigkeit bei nicht interaktiven Bereitstellungen beeinflusst.

es.title: Efectos de encuadre en modelos de lenguaje de agentes independientes: Un análisis conductual entre familias
es.summary: Estudia cómo el encuadre de indicaciones influye en la toma de decisiones del LLM en configuraciones multiagente independientes, encontrando que las pistas lingüísticas desplazan significativamente las preferencias hacia opciones adversas al riesgo. Destaca el encuadre como una fuente de sesgo crítica que afecta la alineación y confiabilidad en despliegues no interactivos.

title: CDEoH: Category-Driven Automatic Algorithm Design With Large Language Models
summary: Proposes CDEoH to improve stability in LLM-based algorithm generation by explicitly modeling algorithm categories and balancing performance with diversity. Demonstrates enhanced evolutionary stability and superior performance on combinatorial optimization across multiple scales.
content:
arXiv:2603.19284v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: With the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), LLM-based heuristic search methods have demonstrated strong capabilities in automated algorithm generation. However, their evolutionary processes often suffer from instability and premature convergence. Existing approaches mainly address this issue through prompt engineering or by jointly evolving thought and code, while largely overlooking the critical role of algorithmic category diversity in maintaining evolutionary stability. To this end, we propose Category Driven Automatic Algorithm Design with Large Language Models (CDEoH), which explicitly models algorithm categories and jointly balances performance and category diversity in population management, enabling parallel exploration across multiple algorithmic paradigms. Extensive experiments on representative combinatorial optimization problems across multiple scales demonstrate that CDEoH effectively mitigates convergence toward a single evolutionary direction, significantly enhancing evolutionary stability and achieving consistently superior average performance across tasks and scales.

zh.title: CDEoH：使用大型语言模型进行类别驱动的自动算法设计
zh.summary: 提出CDEoH，通过显式建模算法类别并平衡性能与多样性，提高基于LLM的算法生成的稳定性。在多个规模的组合优化上展示了增强的演化稳定性和优越的性能。

fr.title: CDEoH : Conception automatique d'algorithmes basée sur les catégories avec des modèles de langage de grande taille
fr.summary: Propose CDEoH pour améliorer la stabilité de la génération d'algorithmes basée sur LLM en modélisant explicitement les catégories d'algorithmes et en équilibrant la performance avec la diversité. Démontre une stabilité évolutive améliorée et des performances supérieures sur l'optimisation combinatoire à plusieurs échelles.

de.title: CDEoH: Kategoriegesteuerte automatische Algorithmusdesign mit großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Schlägt CDEoH vor, um die Stabilität bei der LLM-basierten Algorithmusgenerierung durch explizite Modellierung von Algorithmuskategorien und Ausgewogenheit zwischen Leistung und Vielfalt zu verbessern. Demonstriert verbesserte evolutionäre Stabilität und überlegene Leistung bei kombinatorischer Optimierung über mehrere Skalen.

es.title: CDEoH: Diseño de algoritmos automático impulsado por categorías con modelos de lenguaje grande
es.summary: Propone CDEoH para mejorar la estabilidad en la generación de algoritmos basada en LLM mediante el modelado explícito de categorías de algoritmos y el equilibrio entre rendimiento y diversidad. Demuestra estabilidad evolutiva mejorada y rendimiento superior en optimización combinatoria en múltiples escalas.

title: Generalized Stock Price Prediction for Multiple Stocks Combined with News Fusion
summary: Integrates LLMs with daily financial news for stock price prediction using attention mechanisms to filter relevant articles by stock name embeddings. Achieves 7.11% MAE reduction by training a single generalized model applicable across multiple stocks.
content:
arXiv:2603.19286v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Predicting stock prices presents challenges in financial forecasting. While traditional approaches such as ARIMA and RNNs are prevalent, recent developments in Large Language Models (LLMs) offer alternative methodologies. This paper introduces an approach that integrates LLMs with daily financial news for stock price prediction. To address the challenge of processing news data and identifying relevant content, we utilize stock name embeddings within attention mechanisms. Specifically, we encode news articles using a pre-trained LLM and implement three attention-based pooling techniques -- self-attentive, cross-attentive, and position-aware self-attentive pooling -- to filter news based on stock relevance. The filtered news embeddings, combined with historical stock prices, serve as inputs to the prediction model. Unlike prior studies that focus on individual stocks, our method trains a single generalized model applicable across multiple stocks. Experimental results demonstrate a 7.11% reduction in Mean Absolute Error (MAE) compared to the baseline, indicating the utility of stock name embeddings for news filtering and price forecasting within a generalized framework.

zh.title: 多股票新闻融合的通用股价预测
zh.summary: 将大型语言模型与日常财经新闻相结合进行股价预测,使用注意力机制按股票名称嵌入过滤相关文章。通过训练适用于多只股票的单一通用模型,实现7.11%的平均绝对误差降低。

fr.title: Prédiction généralisée du prix des actions pour plusieurs actions combinées avec la fusion de nouvelles
fr.summary: Intègre les LLM avec les actualités financières quotidiennes pour la prédiction du prix des actions en utilisant des mécanismes d'attention pour filtrer les articles pertinents par les plongements des noms d'actions.
