Réalise une réduction de 7,11% de la MAE en entraînant un modèle généralisé unique applicable à plusieurs actions.

de.title: Verallgemeinerte Aktienkursvorhersage für mehrere Aktien mit Nachrichtenfusion
de.summary: Integriert LLMs mit täglichen Finanznachrichten zur Aktienkursvorhersage unter Verwendung von Aufmerksamkeitsmechanismen zur Filterung relevanter Artikel nach Aktienname-Embeddings. Erreicht eine Reduktion des MAE um 7,11% durch Training eines einzelnen verallgemeinerten Modells, das auf mehrere Aktien anwendbar ist.

es.title: Predicción generalizada de precios de acciones para múltiples acciones con fusión de noticias
es.summary: Integra LLMs con noticias financieras diarias para predicción de precios de acciones utilizando mecanismos de atención para filtrar artículos relevantes mediante incrustaciones de nombres de acciones. Logra una reducción del 7,11% en MAE al entrenar un modelo generalizado único aplicable a múltiples acciones.

title: Joint Return and Risk Modeling with Deep Neural Networks for Portfolio Construction
summary: Proposes end-to-end deep learning framework for joint return and risk forecasting in portfolio optimization. Achieves 36.4% annual return and 0.91 Sharpe ratio, demonstrating consistent improvements over traditional mean-variance approaches on large-cap equities.
content:
arXiv:2603.19288v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Portfolio construction traditionally relies on separately estimating expected returns and covariance matrices using historical statistics, often leading to suboptimal allocation under time-varying market conditions. This paper proposes a joint return and risk modeling framework based on deep neural networks that enables end-to-end learning of dynamic expected returns and risk structures from sequential financial data. Using daily data from ten large-cap US equities spanning 2010 to 2024, the proposed model is evaluated across return prediction, risk estimation, and portfolio-level performance. Out-of-sample results during 2020 to 2024 show that the deep forecasting model achieves competitive predictive accuracy (RMSE = 0.0264) with economically meaningful directional accuracy (51.9%). More importantly, the learned representation effectively captures volatility clustering and regime shifts. When integrated into portfolio optimization, the proposed Neural Portfolio strategy achieves an annual return of 36.4% and a Sharpe ratio of 0.91, outperforming equal weight and historical mean-variance benchmarks in terms of risk-adjusted performance. These findings demonstrate that jointly modeling return and covariance dynamics can provide consistent improvements over traditional allocation approaches. The framework offers a scalable and practical alternative for data-driven portfolio construction under nonstationary market conditions.

zh.title: 用于投资组合构建的深度神经网络联合收益与风险建模
zh.summary: 提出了一个端到端深度学习框架,用于投资组合优化中的联合收益和风险预测。在大盘股上实现了36.4%的年收益率和0.91的夏普比率,相比传统均值-方差方法表现出一致的改进。

fr.title: Modélisation conjointe du rendement et du risque avec les réseaux de neurones profonds pour la construction de portefeuille
fr.summary: Propose un cadre d'apprentissage profond de bout en bout pour la prévision conjointe du rendement et du risque dans l'optimisation de portefeuille. Réalise un rendement annuel de 36,4% et un ratio de Sharpe de 0,91, démontrant des améliorations constantes par rapport aux approches traditionnelles de moyenne-variance sur les grandes capitalisations.

de.title: Gemeinsame Modellierung von Rendite und Risiko mit Deep Neural Networks für die Portfoliokonstruktion
de.summary: Schlägt einen End-to-End-Deep-Learning-Rahmen für die gemeinsame Vorhersage von Rendite und Risiko bei der Portfoliooptimierung vor. Erreicht eine jährliche Rendite von 36,4% und ein Sharpe-Verhältnis von 0,91 und zeigt konsistente Verbesserungen gegenüber traditionellen Mittelwert-Varianz-Ansätzen bei Large-Cap-Aktien.

es.title: Modelado conjunto de rentabilidad y riesgo con redes neuronales profundas para la construcción de cartera
es.summary: Propone un marco de aprendizaje profundo de extremo a extremo para la previsión conjunta de rentabilidad y riesgo en la optimización de cartera. Logra una rentabilidad anual del 36,4% y una relación de Sharpe de 0,91, demostrando mejoras consistentes sobre los enfoques tradicionales de media-varianza en acciones de gran capitalización.

title: Speculating Experts Accelerates Inference for Mixture-of-Experts
summary: Introduces expert prefetching scheme that predicts future MoE weights to enable CPU-GPU memory transfer overlap during inference. Achieves up to 14% reduction in decoding latency by overlapping transfers with speculative expert execution in memory-constrained settings.
content:
arXiv:2603.19289v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have gained popularity as a means of scaling the capacity of large language models (LLMs) while maintaining sparse activations and reduced per-token compute. However, in memory-constrained inference settings, expert weights must be offloaded to CPU, creating a performance bottleneck from CPU-GPU transfers during decoding. We propose an expert prefetching scheme that leverages currently computed internal model representations to speculate future experts, enabling memory transfers to overlap with computation. Across multiple MoE architectures, we demonstrate that future experts can be reliably predicted by these internal representations. We also demonstrate that executing speculated experts generally maintains downstream task accuracy, thus preserving more effective compute-memory overlap by eliminating the need to re-fetch true router-selected experts. Integrated into an optimized inference engine, our approach achieves up to 14\% reduction in time per output token (TPOT) over on-demand loading of experts from CPU memory. For MoEs where speculative execution alone yields suboptimal accuracy, we further examine lightweight estimators that improve expert prediction hit rates, thereby reducing performance degradation. Our code is released in open-source at https://github.com/axonn-ai/yalis/tree/offload_prefetch.

zh.title: 推测专家加速混合专家模型推理
zh.summary: 引入专家预取方案,预测未来MoE权重以在推理期间启用CPU-GPU内存传输重叠。通过在内存受限的设置中将传输与推测性专家执行重叠,实现高达14%的解码延迟减少。

fr.title: Les experts spéculatifs accélèrent l'inférence pour le mélange d'experts
fr.summary: Introduit un schéma de prélecture d'experts qui prédit les poids futurs du MoE pour permettre le chevauchement des transferts de mémoire CPU-GPU pendant l'inférence. Réalise une réduction jusqu'à 14% de la latence de décodage en chevauchant les transferts avec l'exécution d'experts spéculatifs dans des paramètres limités en mémoire.

de.title: Spekulierende Experten beschleunigen die Inferenz für Mixture-of-Experts
de.summary: Führt ein Expert-Prefetching-Schema ein, das zukünftige MoE-Gewichte vorhersagt, um die Überlappung von CPU-GPU-Speichertransfers während der Inferenz zu ermöglichen. Erreicht bis zu 14% Reduktion der Dekodierungslatenz durch Überlappung von Transfers mit spekulativer Expertenausführung in speicherbegrenzten Einstellungen.

es.title: Expertos especulativos aceleran la inferencia para Mixture-of-Experts
es.summary: Introduce un esquema de prefetch de expertos que predice los pesos futuros de MoE para habilitar la superposición de transferencia de memoria CPU-GPU durante la inferencia. Logra una reducción de hasta 14% en la latencia de decodificación al superponer transferencias con ejecución especulativa de expertos en configuraciones de memoria limitada.

title: Neural Dynamics Self-Attention for Spiking Transformers
summary: Addresses energy efficiency in Spiking Transformers by proposing LRF-Dyn, which uses localized receptive fields and membrane dynamics to compute attention. Reduces memory overhead while improving performance on vision tasks by eliminating explicit attention matrix storage.
content:
arXiv:2603.19290v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Integrating Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) with Transformer architectures offers a promising pathway to balance energy efficiency and performance, particularly for edge vision applications. However, existing Spiking Transformers face two critical challenges: (i) a substantial performance gap compared to their Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) counterparts and (ii) high memory overhead during inference. Through theoretical analysis, we attribute both limitations to the Spiking Self-Attention (SSA) mechanism: the lack of locality bias and the need to store large attention matrices. Inspired by the localized receptive fields (LRF) and membrane-potential dynamics of biological visual neurons, we propose LRF-Dyn, which uses spiking neurons with localized receptive fields to compute attention while reducing memory requirements. Specifically, we introduce a LRF method into SSA to assign higher weights to neighboring regions, strengthening local modeling and improving performance. Building on this, we approximate the resulting attention computation via charge-fire-reset dynamics, eliminating explicit attention-matrix storage and reducing inference-time memory. Extensive experiments on visual tasks confirm that our method reduces memory overhead while delivering significant performance improvements. These results establish it as a key unit for achieving energy-efficient Spiking Transformers.

zh.title: 神经动力学自注意机制用于尖峰变压器
zh.summary: 通过提出LRF-Dyn来解决尖峰变压器中的能效问题，该方法使用本地感受野和膜动力学来计算注意力。通过消除显式注意矩阵存储，降低内存开销的同时改进了视觉任务的性能。

fr.title: Auto-attention Dynamique Neuronal pour les Transformateurs Spiking
fr.summary: Aborde l'efficacité énergétique des Transformateurs Spiking en proposant LRF-Dyn, qui utilise des champs réceptifs localisés et la dynamique membranaire pour calculer l'attention. Réduit les frais généraux de mémoire tout en améliorant les performances sur les tâches de vision en éliminant le stockage explicite de la matrice d'attention.

de.title: Neuronale Dynamik Self-Attention für Spiking Transformers
de.summary: Befasst sich mit der Energieeffizienz in Spiking Transformers durch das Konzept LRF-Dyn, das lokalisierte rezeptive Felder und Membrandomynamik zur Berechnung der Aufmerksamkeit nutzt. Reduziert Speicheraufwand und verbessert gleichzeitig die Leistung bei Vision-Aufgaben, indem die explizite Speicherung von Aufmerksamkeitsmatrizen eliminiert wird.

es.title: Autoatención Dinámica Neural para Transformadores Spiking
es.summary: Aborda la eficiencia energética en Transformadores Spiking al proponer LRF-Dyn, que utiliza campos receptivos localizados y dinámicas de membrana para calcular la atención. Reduce la sobrecarga de memoria mientras mejora el rendimiento en tareas de visión al eliminar el almacenamiento explícito de matrices de atención.

title: A Visualization for Comparative Analysis of Regression Models
summary: Introduces novel visualization using 2D residual space and Mahalanobis distance to compare regression model performance. Enables identification of error patterns, correlations, and outliers that traditional aggregate metrics obscure.
content:
arXiv:2603.19291v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: As regression is a widely studied problem, many methods have been proposed to solve it, each of them often requiring setting different hyper-parameters. Therefore, selecting the proper method for a given application may be very difficult and relies on comparing their performances. Performance is usually measured using various metrics such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), or R-squared (R${}^2$). These metrics provide a numerical summary of predictive accuracy by quantifying the difference between predicted and actual values. However, while these metrics are widely used in the literature for summarizing model performance and useful to distinguish between models performing poorly and well, they often aggregate too much information. This article addresses these limitations by introducing a novel visualization approach that highlights key aspects of regression model performance. The proposed method builds upon three main contributions: (1) considering the residuals in a 2D space, which allows for simultaneous evaluation of errors from two models, (2) leveraging the Mahalanobis distance to account for correlations and differences in scale within the data, and (3) employing a colormap to visualize the percentile-based distribution of errors, making it easier to identify dense regions and outliers. By graphically representing the distribution of errors and their correlations, this approach provides a more detailed and comprehensive view of model performance, enabling users to uncover patterns that traditional aggregate metrics may obscure. The proposed visualization method facilitates a deeper understanding of regression model performance differences and error distributions, enhancing the evaluation and comparison process.

zh.title: 回归模型比较分析的可视化方法
zh.summary: 介绍了一种使用二维残差空间和马氏距离的新型可视化方法来比较回归模型的性能。能够识别传统聚合指标所隐藏的错误模式、相关性和异常值。

fr.title: Une Visualisation pour l'Analyse Comparative des Modèles de Régression
fr.summary: Introduit une nouvelle visualisation utilisant un espace résiduel 2D et la distance de Mahalanobis pour comparer les performances des modèles de régression. Permet d'identifier les modèles d'erreur, les corrélations et les valeurs aberrantes que les métriques agrégées traditionnelles ne révèlent pas.

de.title: Eine Visualisierung zur vergleichenden Analyse von Regressionsmodellen
de.summary: Stellt eine neuartige Visualisierung vor, die einen 2D-Restbereich und die Mahalanobis-Distanz verwendet, um die Leistung von Regressionsmodellen zu vergleichen. Ermöglicht die Identifikation von Fehlermustern, Korrelationen und Ausreißern, die traditionelle Aggregatmetriken verbergen.

es.title: Una Visualización para el Análisis Comparativo de Modelos de Regresión
es.summary: Introduce una nueva visualización utilizando espacio residual 2D y distancia de Mahalanobis para comparar el rendimiento de modelos de regresión. Permite identificar patrones de error, correlaciones y valores atípicos que las métricas agregadas tradicionales ocultan.

title: Automatic Analysis of Collaboration Through Human Conversational Data Resources: A Review
summary: Reviews methods for analyzing collaborative processes using task-oriented conversational data, covering relevant theories, coding schemes, and modeling approaches. Provides practical guidance on leveraging dialogue data to understand collaboration behaviors.
content:
arXiv:2603.19292v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Collaboration is a task-oriented, high-level human behavior. In most cases, conversation serves as the primary medium for information exchange and coordination, making conversational data a valuable resource for the automatic analysis of collaborative processes. In this paper, we focus on verbal aspects of collaboration and conduct a review of collaboration analysis using task-oriented conversation resources, encompassing related theories, coding schemes, tasks, and modeling approaches. We aim to address the question of how to utilize task-oriented human-human conversational data for collaboration analysis. We hope our review will serve as a practical resource and illuminate unexplored areas for future collaboration analysis.

zh.title: 通过人类对话数据资源自动分析协作：综述
zh.summary: 综述了使用任务导向对话数据分析协作过程的方法，涵盖相关理论、编码方案和建模方法。提供了利用对话数据理解协作行为的实用指导。

fr.title: Analyse Automatique de la Collaboration par les Ressources de Données de Conversation Humaine: Une Revue
fr.summary: Passe en revue les méthodes d'analyse des processus collaboratifs utilisant des données conversationnelles orientées vers les tâches, couvrant les théories pertinentes, les schémas de codage et les approches de modélisation. Fournit des conseils pratiques pour exploiter les données de dialogue afin de comprendre les comportements de collaboration.

de.title: Automatische Analyse der Zusammenarbeit durch Ressourcen für menschliche Gesprächsdaten: Eine Übersicht
de.summary: Überprüft Methoden zur Analyse von Zusammenarbeitsprozessen unter Verwendung von aufgabenorientierten Gesprächsdaten, die relevante Theorien, Kodierungsschemata und Modellierungsansätze abdecken. Bietet praktische Anleitung zur Nutzung von Dialogdaten, um Zusammenarbeitsverhaltensweisen zu verstehen.

es.title: Análisis Automático de la Colaboración a través de Recursos de Datos Conversacionales Humanos: Una Revisión
es.summary: Revisa métodos para analizar procesos colaborativos utilizando datos conversacionales orientados a tareas, cubriendo teorías relevantes, esquemas de codificación y enfoques de modelado. Proporciona orientación práctica para aprovechar datos de diálogo para comprender comportamientos de colaboración.

title: LLM-MRD: LLM-Guided Multi-View Reasoning Distillation for Fake News Detection
summary: Proposes LLM-MRD, a teacher-student framework combining textual, visual, and cross-modal perspectives for fake news detection. Achieves 5.19% average accuracy improvement by distilling complex LLM reasoning into efficient student models.
content:
arXiv:2603.19293v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Multimodal fake news detection is crucial for mitigating societal disinformation. Existing approaches attempt to address this by fusing multimodal features or leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) for advanced reasoning. However, these methods suffer from serious limitations, including a lack of comprehensive multi-view judgment and fusion, and prohibitive reasoning inefficiency due to the high computational costs of LLMs. To address these issues, we propose \textbf{LLM}-Guided \textbf{M}ulti-View \textbf{R}easoning \textbf{D}istillation for Fake News Detection ( \textbf{LLM-MRD}), a novel teacher-student framework. The Student Multi-view Reasoning module first constructs a comprehensive foundation from textual, visual, and cross-modal perspectives. Then, the Teacher Multi-view Reasoning module generates deep reasoning chains as rich supervision signals. Our core Calibration Distillation mechanism efficiently distills this complex reasoning-derived knowledge into the efficient student model. Experiments show LLM-MRD significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Notably, it demonstrates a comprehensive average improvement of 5.19\% in ACC and 6.33\% in F1-Fake when evaluated across all competing methods and datasets. Our code is available at https://github.com/Nasuro55/LLM-MRD

zh.title: LLM-MRD：用于假新闻检测的LLM引导多视角推理蒸馏
zh.summary: 提出LLM-MRD，一个结合文本、视觉和跨模态视角的师生框架，用于假新闻检测。通过将复杂的LLM推理蒸馏到高效的学生模型中，实现了平均准确度提高5.19%。

fr.title: LLM-MRD : Distillation du raisonnement multi-vue guidé par LLM pour la détection de fausses nouvelles
fr.summary: Propose LLM-MRD, un cadre enseignant-étudiant combinant les perspectives textuelles, visuelles et multimodales pour la détection de fausses nouvelles. Atteint une amélioration de la précision moyenne de 5,19% en distillant le raisonnement complexe des LLM dans des modèles étudiants efficaces.

de.title: LLM-MRD: LLM-gesteuertes Multi-View-Reasoning-Destillation zur Erkennung von Fake News
de.summary: Stellt LLM-MRD vor, ein Lehrer-Schüler-Framework, das textuelle, visuelle und Cross-Modal-Perspektiven zur Erkennung von Fake News kombiniert. Erreicht durch Destillation komplexer LLM-Reasoning in effiziente Schülermodelle eine durchschnittliche Verbesserung der Genauigkeit um 5,19%.

es.title: LLM-MRD: Destilación de razonamiento multi-vista guiada por LLM para la detección de noticias falsas
es.summary: Propone LLM-MRD, un marco maestro-estudiante que combina perspectivas textuales, visuales y multimodales para la detección de noticias falsas. Logra una mejora promedio de precisión del 5,19% al destilar el razonamiento complejo de LLM en modelos estudiantiles eficientes.

title: Maximizing mutual information between user-contexts and responses improve LLM personalization with no additional data
summary: Introduces MIPO, a contrastive data augmentation method for LLM personalization using mutual information maximization without additional labeled data. Shows 3-40% improvements on personalization tasks and 1-18% gains on math problems without external supervision.
content:
arXiv:2603.19294v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While post-training has successfully improved large language models (LLMs) across a variety of domains, these gains heavily rely on human-labeled data or external verifiers. Existing data has already been exploited, and new high-quality data is expensive to collect. More fundamentally, true intelligence goes far beyond tasks that are easily verifiable. Therefore, we need self-improvement frameworks that allow models to improve without external oversight. We propose *Mutual Information Preference Optimization (MIPO)*, a contrastive data augmentation method that constructs preference pairs by generating a positive response conditioning on the correct prompt, and a negative response by conditioning on a random, unrelated prompt. We show that using Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) to learn from this paired data maximizes pointwise conditional mutual information (MI) (under the base LLM) between prompts and model responses. Empirical results with various-sized Llama- and Qwen-Instruct models show that when used to maximize MI between user context and response, MIPO provides an effective personalization technique, achieving 3-40% improvements on personalization tasks using real-user datasets compared to strong baselines. Surprisingly, MIPO can also be applied to improve performance on math and multiple-choice problems, yielding 1-18% **without any additional data or human supervision**. These results suggest a promising direction for self-improvement.

zh.title: 最大化用户上下文和响应之间的互信息以改进LLM个性化，无需额外数据
zh.summary: 介绍MIPO，一种使用互信息最大化的对比数据增强方法，用于LLM个性化，无需额外标记数据。在个性化任务上显示3-40%的改进，在数学问题上显示1-18%的增益，无需外部监督。

fr.title: Maximiser l'information mutuelle entre les contextes utilisateur et les réponses pour améliorer la personnalisation des LLM sans données supplémentaires
fr.summary: Introduit MIPO, une méthode d'augmentation de données contrastive pour la personnalisation des LLM utilisant la maximisation de l'information mutuelle sans données étiquetées supplémentaires. Montre des améliorations de 3-40% sur les tâches de personnalisation et des gains de 1-18% sur les problèmes mathématiques sans supervision externe.

de.title: Maximierung der gegenseitigen Information zwischen Benutzerkontexten und Antworten zur Verbesserung der LLM-Personalisierung ohne zusätzliche Daten
de.summary: Stellt MIPO vor, eine kontrastive Datenerweiterungsmethode zur LLM-Personalisierung unter Verwendung von gegenseitiger Informationsmaximierung ohne zusätzliche beschriftete Daten. Zeigt Verbesserungen von 3-40% bei Personalisierungsaufgaben und Gewinne von 1-18% bei mathematischen Problemen ohne externe Überwachung.

es.title: Maximizar la información mutua entre contextos de usuario y respuestas para mejorar la personalización de LLM sin datos adicionales
es.summary: Introduce MIPO, un método de aumento de datos contrastivos para personalización de LLM usando maximización de información mutua sin datos etiquetados adicionales. Muestra mejoras del 3-40% en tareas de personalización y ganancias del 1-18% en problemas matemáticos sin supervisión externa.

title: Parameter-Efficient Token Embedding Editing for Clinical Class-Level Unlearning
summary: Introduces STEU, a parameter-efficient method for machine unlearning in clinical language models that modifies only token embeddings. Achieves near-complete forgetting while preserving utility by editing just 0.19% of parameters.
content:
arXiv:2603.19302v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Machine unlearning is increasingly important for clinical language models, where privacy regulations and institutional policies may require removing sensitive information from deployed systems without retraining from scratch. In practice, deletion requests must balance effective forgetting of targeted information with preservation of model utility and minimal parameter modification. We introduce Sparse Token Embedding Unlearning (STEU), a parameter-efficient method for behavioral class-level unlearning that updates only PMI-selected token embeddings together with a small classifier head while keeping all encoder layers frozen. Across experiments on MIMIC-IV, MIMIC-III, and eICU using BioClinicalBERT, BERT-base, and DistilBERT, STEU consistently suppresses the target class while largely preserving retained task performance. In the primary MIMIC-IV setting, STEU achieves near-complete forgetting (forget F1 = 0.0004) while maintaining competitive retained utility (retain avg F1 = 0.4766) after modifying only 0.19\% of model parameters. These results suggest that targeted behavioral unlearning can be achieved through sparse embedding edits without modifying deeper encoder representations.

zh.title: 参数高效的令牌嵌入编辑用于临床类级别的机器遗忘
zh.summary: 引入STEU，一种用于临床语言模型中机器遗忘的参数高效方法，仅修改令牌嵌入。通过仅编辑0.19%的参数，实现近乎完全的遗忘，同时保持效用。

fr.title: Édition d'embeddings de jetons efficace en paramètres pour l'oubli machine au niveau des classes cliniques
fr.summary: Introduit STEU, une méthode efficace en paramètres pour l'apprentissage machine involontaire dans les modèles de langue cliniques qui modifie uniquement les embeddings de jetons. Atteint un oubli quasi-complet tout en préservant l'utilité en éditant seulement 0,19% des paramètres.

de.title: Parametereffiziente Token-Embedding-Bearbeitung für klinisches klassenübergreifendes Vergessen
de.summary: Stellt STEU vor, eine parametereffiziente Methode zum maschinellen Vergessen in klinischen Sprachmodellen, die nur Token-Embeddings modifiziert. Erreicht ein nahezu vollständiges Vergessen unter Beibehaltung der Nützlichkeit durch Bearbeitung von nur 0,19% der Parameter.

es.title: Edición de incrustación de tokens eficiente en parámetros para la supresión de nivel de clase clínica
es.summary: Introduce STEU, un método eficiente en parámetros para la supresión involuntaria de aprendizaje automático en modelos de lenguaje clínico que modifica solo incrustaciones de tokens. Logra un olvido casi completo mientras preserva la utilidad al editar solo el 0,19% de los parámetros.

title: Agreement Between Large Language Models, Human Reviewers, and Authors in Evaluating STROBE Checklists for Observational Studies in Rheumatology
summary: Compares LLM, human, and author assessments of STROBE reporting compliance across 17 rheumatology studies, finding 85% overall agreement. LLMs perform well on formatting but show lower reliability on complex methodological evaluations requiring expert judgment.
content:
arXiv:2603.19303v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Introduction: Evaluating compliance with the Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology (STROBE) statement can be time-consuming and subjective. This study compares STROBE assessments from large language models (LLMs), a human reviewer panel, and the original manuscript authors in observational rheumatology research. Methods: Guided by the GRRAS and DEAL Pathway B frameworks, 17 rheumatology articles were independently assessed. Evaluations used the 22-item STROBE checklist, completed by the authors, a five-person human panel (ranging from junior to senior professionals), and two LLMs (ChatGPT-5.2, Gemini-3Pro). Items were grouped into Methodological Rigor and Presentation and Context domains. Inter-rater reliability was calculated using Gwet's Agreement Coefficient (AC1). Results: Overall agreement across all reviewers was 85.0% (AC1=0.826). Domain stratification showed almost perfect agreement for Presentation and Context (AC1=0.841) and substantial agreement for Methodological Rigor (AC1=0.803). Although LLMs achieved complete agreement (AC1=1.000) with all human reviewers on standard formatting elements, their agreement with human reviewers and authors declined on complex items. For example, regarding the item on loss to follow-up, the agreement between Gemini 3 Pro and the senior reviewer was AC1=-0.252, while the agreement with the authors was only fair. Additionally, ChatGPT-5.2 generally demonstrated higher agreement with human reviewers than Gemini-3Pro on specific methodological items. Conclusion: While LLMs show potential for basic STROBE screening, their lower agreement with human experts on complex methodological items likely reflects a reliance on surface-level information. Currently, these models appear more reliable for standardizing straightforward checks than for replacing expert human judgment in evaluating observational research.

zh.title: 大语言模型、人类评审员和作者在评估风湿病观察性研究STROBE清单中的一致性
zh.summary: 比较了大语言模型、人类和作者对17项风湿病研究中STROBE报告合规性的评估，发现整体一致性为85%。大语言模型在格式方面表现良好，但在需要专家判断的复杂方法论评估中可靠性较低。

fr.title: Accord entre les grands modèles de langage, les examinateurs humains et les auteurs dans l'évaluation des listes de contrôle STROBE pour les études observationnelles en rhumatologie
fr.summary: Compare les évaluations des modèles de langage de grande taille, des humains et des auteurs sur la conformité aux rapports STROBE dans 17 études de rhumatologie, trouvant un accord global de 85%. Les modèles de langage de grande taille performent bien sur le formatage mais montrent une fiabilité inférieure sur les évaluations méthodologiques complexes nécessitant un jugement d'expert.

de.title: Übereinstimmung zwischen großen Sprachmodellen, menschlichen Reviewern und Autoren bei der Bewertung von STROBE-Checklisten für Beobachtungsstudien in der Rheumatologie
de.summary: Vergleicht die Bewertungen von LLMs, Menschen und Autoren zur STROBE-Berichterstattungskonformität in 17 Rheuma-Studien und stellt eine Gesamtübereinstimmung von 85% fest. LLMs sind bei der Formatierung gut, zeigen aber eine geringere Zuverlässigkeit bei komplexen methodologischen Bewertungen, die Expertenbewertung erfordern.

es.title: Acuerdo entre grandes modelos de lenguaje, revisores humanos y autores en la evaluación de listas de verificación STROBE para estudios observacionales en reumatología
es.summary: Compara las evaluaciones de LLM, humanos y autores sobre el cumplimiento de informes STROBE en 17 estudios de reumatología, encontrando un acuerdo general del 85%. Los LLM funcionan bien en el formato pero muestran una confiabilidad menor en evaluaciones metodológicas complejas que requieren juicio de expertos.

title: PhyGile: Physics-Prefix Guided Motion Generation for Agile General Humanoid Motion Tracking
summary: Presents PhyGile, a framework generating physically feasible humanoid robot motions guided by physics prefixes at inference time. Enables stable execution of complex, agile whole-body motions on real robots beyond traditional walking and low-dynamic movements.
content:
arXiv:2603.19305v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Humanoid robots are expected to execute agile and expressive whole-body motions in real-world settings. Existing text-to-motion generation models are predominantly trained on captured human motion datasets, whose priors assume human biomechanics, actuation, mass distribution, and contact strategies. When such motions are directly retargeted to humanoid robots, the resulting trajectories may satisfy geometric constraints (e.g., joint limits and pose continuity) and appear kinematically reasonable. However, they frequently violate the physical feasibility required for real-world execution. To address these issues, we present PhyGile, a unified framework that closes the loop between robot-native motion generation and General Motion Tracking (GMT). PhyGile performs physics-prefix-guided robot-native motion generation at inference time, directly generating robot-native motions in a 262-dimensional skeletal space with physics-guided prefixes, thereby eliminating inference-time retargeting artifacts and reducing generation-execution discrepancies. Before physics-prefix adaptation, we train the GMT controller with a curriculum-based mixture-of-experts scheme, followed by post-training on unlabeled motion data to improve robustness over large-scale robot motions. During physics-prefix adaptation, the GMT controller is further fine-tuned with generated objectives under physics-derived prefixes, enabling agile and stable execution of complex motions on real robots. Extensive offline and real-robot experiments demonstrate that PhyGile expands the frontier of text-driven humanoid control, enabling stable tracking of agile, highly difficult whole-body motions that go well beyond walking and low-dynamic motions typically achieved by prior methods.

zh.title: PhyGile：物理前缀引导的敏捷通用人形机器人运动生成
zh.summary: 介绍PhyGile，一个在推理时由物理前缀指导生成物理可行的人形机器人运动的框架。使真实机器人能够稳定执行复杂、敏捷的全身运动，超越传统行走和低动态运动。

fr.title: PhyGile : Génération de mouvements guidée par préfixe physique pour le suivi de mouvements généraux agiles humanoides
fr.summary: Présente PhyGile, un cadre générant des mouvements de robots humanoïdes physiquement réalisables guidés par des préfixes physiques au moment de l'inférence. Permet l'exécution stable de mouvements complexes et agiles du corps entier sur des robots réels au-delà des mouvements de marche traditionnels et à faible dynamique.

de.title: PhyGile: Physik-Präfix-geleitete Bewegungsgenerierung für wendige allgemeine humanoidale Bewegungsverfolgung
de.summary: Präsentiert PhyGile, ein Framework zur Generierung physikalisch realisierbarer Bewegungen humanoidaler Roboter, gelenkt durch Physik-Präfixe zur Inferenzzeit. Ermöglicht die stabile Ausführung komplexer, wendiger Ganzkörperbewegungen auf echten Robotern über traditionelle Geh- und Niedrigdynamikbewegungen hinaus.

es.title: PhyGile: Generación de movimiento guiada por prefijo físico para rastreo de movimiento humanoide general ágil
es.summary: Presenta PhyGile, un marco que genera movimientos de robots humanoides físicamente viables guiados por prefijos físicos en tiempo de inferencia. Permite la ejecución estable de movimientos complejos y ágiles de cuerpo completo en robots reales más allá de los movimientos de caminata tradicionales y de baja dinámica.

title: VERDICT: Verifiable Evolving Reasoning with Directive-Informed Collegial Teams for Legal Judgment Prediction
summary: Proposes VERDICT, a multi-agent framework for legal judgment prediction with traceable reasoning and collaborative verification workflows. Introduces Hybrid Jurisprudential Memory for continual learning, achieving state-of-the-art results with strong temporal generalization.
content:
arXiv:2603.19306v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Legal Judgment Prediction (LJP) predicts applicable law articles, charges, and penalty terms from case facts. Beyond accuracy, LJP calls for intrinsically interpretable and legally grounded reasoning that can reconcile statutory rules with precedent-informed standards. However, existing methods often behave as static, one-shot predictors, providing limited procedural support for verifiable reasoning and little capability to adapt as jurisprudential practice evolves. We propose VERDICT, a self-refining collaborative multi-agent framework that simulates a virtual collegial panel. VERDICT assigns specialized agents to complementary roles (e.g., fact structuring, legal retrieval, opinion drafting, and supervisory verification) and coordinates them in a traceable draft--verify--revise workflow with explicit Pass/Reject feedback, producing verifiable reasoning traces and revision rationales. To capture evolving case experience, we further introduce a Hybrid Jurisprudential Memory (HJM) grounded in the Micro-Directive Paradigm, which stores precedent standards and continually distills validated multi-agent verification trajectories into updated Micro-Directives for continual learning across cases. We evaluate VERDICT on CAIL2018 and a newly constructed CJO2025 dataset with a strict future time-split for temporal generalization. VERDICT achieves state-of-the-art performance on CAIL2018 and demonstrates strong generalization on CJO2025. To facilitate reproducibility and further research, we release our code and the dataset at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ARR-4437.

zh.title: VERDICT：用于法律判决预测的可验证演化推理与指令引导的同事团队框架
zh.summary: 提出VERDICT，一个具有可追踪推理和协作验证工作流的法律判决预测多智能体框架。引入混合法理记忆以实现持续学习，在强时间泛化中取得最先进的结果。

fr.title: VERDICT : Raisonnement évolutif vérifiable avec des équipes collégiales informées par directives pour la prédiction du jugement juridique
fr.summary: Propose VERDICT, un cadre multi-agents pour la prédiction du jugement juridique avec un raisonnement traçable et des flux de travail de vérification collaborative. Introduit une mémoire jurisprudentielle hybride pour l'apprentissage continu, atteignant des résultats de pointe avec une généralisation temporelle forte.

de.title: VERDICT: Verifizierbares evolutionäres Denken mit direktiveninformierten kollegialen Teams zur Vorhersage von Rechtsurteilen
de.summary: Schlägt VERDICT vor, ein Multi-Agent-Framework zur Vorhersage von Rechtsurteilen mit nachverfolgbarem Denken und kollaborativen Verifizierungsabläufen. Führt ein hybrids juristisches Gedächtnis für kontinuierliches Lernen ein und erreicht modernste Ergebnisse mit starker zeitlicher Verallgemeinerung.

es.title: VERDICT: Razonamiento evolutivo verificable con equipos colegiales informados por directivas para la predicción de sentencias legales
es.summary: Propone VERDICT, un marco multiagente para la predicción de sentencias legales con razonamiento trazable y flujos de trabajo de verificación colaborativa. Introduce Memoria Jurisprudencial Híbrida para aprendizaje continuo, logrando resultados de vanguardia con fuerte generalización temporal.
